title.zh: 卷二十七上 五行志
title.en: Volume 27a: Treatise on the Five Elements 1
title.raw: 卷二十七上 五行志 Volume 27a: Treatise on the Five Elements 1

sentences.zh: 《易》曰：「天垂象，見吉凶，聖人象之；

translations.literal: The Changes says: "Heaven hangs down signs to show auspicious and inauspicious; sages model them.
translations.idiomatic: The Changes says: "Heaven hangs down signs to show auspicious and inauspicious; sages model them.

sentences.zh: 河出圖，雒出書，聖人則之。」

translations.literal: The Yellow River produced the Diagram, the Luo produced the Writing, and sages took them as norms."
translations.idiomatic: The Yellow River produced the Diagram, the Luo produced the Writing, and sages took them as norms."

sentences.zh: 劉歆以為虙羲氏繼天而王，受河圖，則而畫之，八卦是也；

translations.literal: Liu Xin held that Fuxi succeeded Heaven and ruled, received the River Diagram, modeled and drew it; this is the Eight Trigrams.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin held that Fuxi succeeded Heaven and ruled, received the River Diagram, modeled and drew it; this is the Eight Trigrams.

sentences.zh: 禹治洪水，賜雒書，法而陳之，洪範是也。

translations.literal: When Yu controlled the great flood, he was granted the Luo Writing, modeled and arranged it; this is the Great Plan.
translations.idiomatic: When Yu controlled the great flood, he was granted the Luo Writing, modeled and arranged it; this is the Great Plan.

sentences.zh: 聖人行其道而寶其真。

translations.literal: Sages practice its Way and treasure its truth.
translations.idiomatic: Sages practice its Way and treasure its truth.

sentences.zh: 降及于殷，箕子在父師位而典之。

translations.literal: By Shang times, Jizi held the rank of grand preceptor and preserved it.
translations.idiomatic: By Shang times, Jizi held the rank of grand preceptor and preserved it.

sentences.zh: 周既克殷，以箕子歸，武王親虛己而問焉。

translations.literal: After Zhou conquered Shang and Jizi returned, King Wu emptied himself and personally asked him.
translations.idiomatic: After Zhou conquered Shang and Jizi returned, King Wu emptied himself and personally asked him.

sentences.zh: 故經曰：「惟十有三祀，王訪于箕子，王乃言曰：『烏呼，箕子！

translations.literal: Thus the Classic says: "In the thirteenth sacrifice year, the king consulted Jizi, and said: 'Ah, Jizi!
translations.idiomatic: Thus the Classic says: "In the thirteenth sacrifice year, the king consulted Jizi, and said: 'Ah, Jizi!

sentences.zh: 惟天陰騭下民，相協厥居，我不知其彝倫逌敘。』

translations.literal: Heaven silently stabilizes the people and harmonizes their dwelling, yet I do not know how constant relations are to be properly ordered.'
translations.idiomatic: Heaven silently stabilizes the people and harmonizes their dwelling, yet I do not know how constant relations are to be properly ordered.'

sentences.zh: 箕子乃言曰：『我聞在昔，鯀讯洪水，汨陳其五行，帝乃震怒，弗畀洪範九疇，彝倫逌斁。

translations.literal: Jizi then said: 'I have heard that in former times Gun blocked the flood and disordered the Five Phases; the Thearch was enraged and did not grant the Nine Categories of the Great Plan, so constant relations collapsed.
translations.idiomatic: Jizi then said: 'I have heard that in former times Gun blocked the flood and disordered the Five Phases; the Thearch was enraged and did not grant the Nine Categories of the Great Plan, so constant relations collapsed.

sentences.zh: 鯀則殛死，禹乃嗣興，天乃錫禹洪範九疇，彝倫逌敘。』」

translations.literal: Gun was then executed in death, and Yu succeeded; Heaven then bestowed on Yu the Nine Categories of the Great Plan, and constant relations were ordered.'"
translations.idiomatic: Gun was then executed in death, and Yu succeeded; Heaven then bestowed on Yu the Nine Categories of the Great Plan, and constant relations were ordered.'"

sentences.zh: 此武王問雒書於箕子，箕子對禹得雒書之意也。

translations.literal: This is King Wu asking Jizi about the Luo Writing, and Jizi answering in the sense of Yu receiving it.
translations.idiomatic: This is King Wu asking Jizi about the Luo Writing, and Jizi answering in the sense of Yu receiving it.

sentences.zh: 「初一曰五行；

translations.literal: "First: the Five Phases.
translations.idiomatic: "First: the Five Phases.

sentences.zh: 次二曰羞用五事；

translations.literal: Second: respectful use of five affairs.
translations.idiomatic: Second: respectful use of five affairs.

sentences.zh: 次三曰農用八政；

translations.literal: Third: proper use of eight policies.
translations.idiomatic: Third: proper use of eight policies.

sentences.zh: 次四曰渖用五紀；

translations.literal: Fourth: careful use of five measures of time.
translations.idiomatic: Fourth: careful use of five measures of time.

sentences.zh: 次五曰建用皇極；

translations.literal: Fifth: establish and use the royal ultimate.
translations.idiomatic: Fifth: establish and use the royal ultimate.

sentences.zh: 次六曰艾用三德；

translations.literal: Sixth: examine and use three virtues.
translations.idiomatic: Sixth: examine and use three virtues.

sentences.zh: 次七曰明用稽疑；

translations.literal: Seventh: clarify and use divination of doubts.
translations.idiomatic: Seventh: clarify and use divination of doubts.

sentences.zh: 次八曰念用庶徵；

translations.literal: Eighth: consider and use various signs.
translations.idiomatic: Eighth: consider and use various signs.

sentences.zh: 次九曰嚮用五福，畏用六極。」

translations.literal: Ninth: direct use of five blessings and fear use of six extremes."
translations.idiomatic: Ninth: direct use of five blessings and fear use of six extremes."

sentences.zh: 凡此六十五字，皆雒書本文，所謂天乃錫禹大法九章常事所次者也。

translations.literal: These sixty-five characters are all original text of the Luo Writing, the sequence of nine chapters of great norms and constant affairs that Heaven bestowed on Yu.
translations.idiomatic: These sixty-five characters are all original text of the Luo Writing, the sequence of nine chapters of great norms and constant affairs that Heaven bestowed on Yu.

sentences.zh: 以為河圖、雒書相為經緯，八卦、九章相為表裏。

translations.literal: Thus River Diagram and Luo Writing are warp and weft to each other, and Eight Trigrams and Nine Chapters are exterior and interior to each other.
translations.idiomatic: Thus River Diagram and Luo Writing are warp and weft to each other, and Eight Trigrams and Nine Chapters are exterior and interior to each other.

sentences.zh: 昔殷道弛，文王演周易；

translations.literal: When Shang's Way slackened, King Wen elaborated the Zhou Changes.
translations.idiomatic: When Shang's Way slackened, King Wen elaborated the Zhou Changes.

sentences.zh: 周道敝，孔子述春秋。

translations.literal: When Zhou's Way decayed, Confucius wrote the Spring and Autumn.
translations.idiomatic: When Zhou's Way decayed, Confucius wrote the Spring and Autumn.

sentences.zh: 則乾坤之陰陽，效洪範之咎徵，天人之道粲然著矣。

translations.literal: Then yin and yang of Qian and Kun and the fault-signs of Great Plan became luminously manifest as the Way of Heaven and humanity.
translations.idiomatic: Then yin and yang of Qian and Kun and the fault-signs of Great Plan became luminously manifest as the Way of Heaven and humanity.

sentences.zh: 漢興，承秦滅學之後，景、武之世，董仲舒治公羊春秋，始推陰陽，為儒者宗。

translations.literal: After Han arose from Qin's destruction of learning, in the reigns of Jing and Wu, Dong Zhongshu studied Gongyang Spring and Autumn and first pushed yin-yang interpretation, becoming model for Confucians.
translations.idiomatic: After Han arose from Qin's destruction of learning, in the reigns of Jing and Wu, Dong Zhongshu studied Gongyang Spring and Autumn and first pushed yin-yang interpretation, becoming model for Confucians.

sentences.zh: 宣、元之後，劉向治穀梁春秋，數其禍福，傳以洪範，與仲舒錯。

translations.literal: After Xuan and Yuan, Liu Xiang studied Guliang Spring and Autumn, counted calamities and blessings, and transmitted them through Great Plan, differing from Zhongshu.
translations.idiomatic: After Xuan and Yuan, Liu Xiang studied Guliang Spring and Autumn, counted calamities and blessings, and transmitted them through Great Plan, differing from Zhongshu.

sentences.zh: 至向子歆治左氏傳，其春秋意亦已乖矣；

translations.literal: Then Xiang's son Xin studied Zuo Tradition, and its Spring and Autumn meaning had already diverged.
translations.idiomatic: Then Xiang's son Xin studied Zuo Tradition, and its Spring and Autumn meaning had already diverged.

sentences.zh: 言五行傳，又頗不同。

translations.literal: His account of Five Phases transmission was also somewhat different.
translations.idiomatic: His account of Five Phases transmission was also somewhat different.

sentences.zh: 是以髓仲舒，別向、歆，傳載眭孟、夏侯勝、京房、谷永、李尋之徒所陳行事，訖於王莽，舉十二世，以傅春秋，著於篇。

translations.literal: Therefore this text follows Zhongshu's core while distinguishing Xiang and Xin, and records statements and actions presented by Sui Meng, Xiahou Sheng, Jing Fang, Gu Yong, Li Xun, and others, down to Wang Mang, covering twelve reigns to transmit and attach to Spring and Autumn.
translations.idiomatic: Therefore this text follows Zhongshu's core while distinguishing Xiang and Xin, and records statements and actions presented by Sui Meng, Xiahou Sheng, Jing Fang, Gu Yong, Li Xun, and others, down to Wang Mang, covering twelve reigns to transmit and attach to Spring and Autumn.

sentences.zh: 經曰：「初一曰五行。

translations.literal: The Classic says: "First is the Five Phases.
translations.idiomatic: The Classic says: "First is the Five Phases.

sentences.zh: 五行：一曰水，二曰火，三曰木，四曰金，五曰土。

translations.literal: The Five Phases: first water, second fire, third wood, fourth metal, fifth earth.
translations.idiomatic: The Five Phases: first water, second fire, third wood, fourth metal, fifth earth.

sentences.zh: 水曰潤下，火曰炎上，木曰曲直，金曰從革，土爰稼穡。」

translations.literal: Water moistens downward, fire blazes upward, wood bends and straightens, metal yields to reforming, earth is for sowing and reaping."
translations.idiomatic: Water moistens downward, fire blazes upward, wood bends and straightens, metal yields to reforming, earth is for sowing and reaping."

sentences.zh: 傳曰：「田獵不宿，飲食不享，出入不節，奪民農時，及有姦謀，則木不曲直。」

translations.literal: The Tradition says: "If hunting does not lodge overnight, food and drink are not ritually offered, movement lacks measure, farming seasons are seized from the people, and there are treacherous plots, then wood does not bend and straighten."
translations.idiomatic: The Tradition says: "If hunting does not lodge overnight, food and drink are not ritually offered, movement lacks measure, farming seasons are seized from the people, and there are treacherous plots, then wood does not bend and straighten."

sentences.zh: 說曰：木，東方也。

translations.literal: Explanation says: Wood is the east.
translations.idiomatic: Explanation says: Wood is the east.

sentences.zh: 於易，地上之木為觀。

translations.literal: In the Changes, wood on the earth is the hexagram Contemplation.
translations.idiomatic: In the Changes, wood on the earth is the hexagram Contemplation.

sentences.zh: 其於王事，威儀容貌亦可觀者也。

translations.literal: In royal affairs, this means demeanor and bearing must be fit to be observed.
translations.idiomatic: In royal affairs, this means demeanor and bearing must be fit to be observed.

sentences.zh: 故行步有佩玉之度，登車有和鸞之節，田狩有三驅之制，飲食有享獻之禮，出入有名，使民以時，務在勸農桑，謀在安百姓：如此，則木得其性矣。

translations.literal: Thus walking has the measure of jade pendants, mounting carriages the rhythm of harmonizing bells; hunting has the rule of three drives; food and drink have rites of presentation; entry and exit have named procedures; people are employed in proper seasons; effort is in encouraging farming and sericulture; plans are in securing the people - then wood obtains its nature.
translations.idiomatic: Thus walking has the measure of jade pendants, mounting carriages the rhythm of harmonizing bells; hunting has the rule of three drives; food and drink have rites of presentation; entry and exit have named procedures; people are employed in proper seasons; effort is in encouraging farming and sericulture; plans are in securing the people - then wood obtains its nature.

sentences.zh: 若乃田獵馳騁不反宮室，飲食沈湎不顧法度，妄興繇役以奪民時，作為姦詐以傷民財，則木失其性矣。

translations.literal: But if one hunts and races without returning to palace, indulges in food and drink disregarding law, rashly raises corvee to seize farming time, and creates fraud that harms people's wealth, then wood loses its nature.
translations.idiomatic: But if one hunts and races without returning to palace, indulges in food and drink disregarding law, rashly raises corvee to seize farming time, and creates fraud that harms people's wealth, then wood loses its nature.

sentences.zh: 蓋工匠之為輪矢者多傷敗，及木為變怪，是為木不曲直。

translations.literal: Then craftsmen making wheels and arrows suffer many breakages and woods produce strange anomalies - this is wood not bending and straightening.
translations.idiomatic: Then craftsmen making wheels and arrows suffer many breakages and woods produce strange anomalies - this is wood not bending and straightening.

sentences.zh: 春秋成公十六年「正月，雨，木冰」。

translations.literal: Spring and Autumn, Cheng year sixteen: "In first month, it rained and trees froze."
translations.idiomatic: Spring and Autumn, Cheng year sixteen: "In first month, it rained and trees froze."

sentences.zh: 劉歆以為上陽施不下通，下陰施不上達，故雨，而木為之冰，雰氣寒，木不曲直也。

translations.literal: Liu Xin held that upper yang influence failed to connect downward and lower yin failed to reach upward, thus rain occurred and trees froze; cold miasma prevailed, wood not bending and straightening.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin held that upper yang influence failed to connect downward and lower yin failed to reach upward, thus rain occurred and trees froze; cold miasma prevailed, wood not bending and straightening.

sentences.zh: 劉向以為冰者陰之盛而水滯者也，木者少陽，貴臣卿大夫之象也。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held that ice is excess yin and stagnated water; wood is lesser yang, image of high ministers and grandees.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held that ice is excess yin and stagnated water; wood is lesser yang, image of high ministers and grandees.

sentences.zh: 此人將有害，則陰氣協木，木先寒，故得雨而冰也。

translations.literal: When these persons are about to be harmed, yin qi couples with wood; wood first becomes cold, thus rain yields freezing.
translations.idiomatic: When these persons are about to be harmed, yin qi couples with wood; wood first becomes cold, thus rain yields freezing.

sentences.zh: 是時叔孫喬如出奔，公子偃誅死。

translations.literal: At that time Shusun Qiaoru fled and Prince Yan was executed.
translations.idiomatic: At that time Shusun Qiaoru fled and Prince Yan was executed.

sentences.zh: 一曰，時晉執季孫行父，又執公，此執辱之異。

translations.literal: Another view: at that time Jin detained Jisun Xingfu and also detained the duke; this was anomaly of capture and humiliation.
translations.idiomatic: Another view: at that time Jin detained Jisun Xingfu and also detained the duke; this was anomaly of capture and humiliation.

sentences.zh: 或曰，今之長老名木冰為「木介」。

translations.literal: Some say elders today call tree-freezing "wood armor."
translations.idiomatic: Some say elders today call tree-freezing "wood armor."

sentences.zh: 介者，甲。

translations.literal: Armor means cuirass.
translations.idiomatic: Armor means cuirass.

sentences.zh: 甲，兵象也。

translations.literal: Cuirass is image of war.
translations.idiomatic: Cuirass is image of war.

sentences.zh: 是歲晉有鄢陵之戰，楚王傷目而敗。

translations.literal: That year Jin fought at Yanling; the king of Chu was wounded in the eye and defeated.
translations.idiomatic: That year Jin fought at Yanling; the king of Chu was wounded in the eye and defeated.

sentences.zh: 屬常雨也。

translations.literal: This belonged to persistent rains.
translations.idiomatic: This belonged to persistent rains.

sentences.zh: 傳曰：「棄法律，逐功臣，殺太子，以妾為妻，則火不炎上。」

translations.literal: Tradition says: "If laws are abandoned, meritorious ministers expelled, crown prince killed, and concubine made wife, then fire does not blaze upward."
translations.idiomatic: Tradition says: "If laws are abandoned, meritorious ministers expelled, crown prince killed, and concubine made wife, then fire does not blaze upward."

sentences.zh: 說曰：火，南方，揚光煇為明者也。

translations.literal: Explanation says: Fire is south, raising brightness for illumination.
translations.idiomatic: Explanation says: Fire is south, raising brightness for illumination.

sentences.zh: 其於王者，南面鄉明而治。

translations.literal: For a ruler, this means facing south toward brightness and governing.
translations.idiomatic: For a ruler, this means facing south toward brightness and governing.

sentences.zh: 《書》云：「知人則悊，能官人。」

translations.literal: The Documents says: "To know men is wisdom; to appoint men is capability."
translations.idiomatic: The Documents says: "To know men is wisdom; to appoint men is capability."

sentences.zh: 故堯舜舉群賢而命之朝，遠四佞而放諸野。

translations.literal: So Yao and Shun raised all worthy men to court and banished four flatterers to the wilds.
translations.idiomatic: So Yao and Shun raised all worthy men to court and banished four flatterers to the wilds.

sentences.zh: 孔子曰：「浸潤之譖、膚受之訴不行焉，可謂明矣。」

translations.literal: Confucius said: "If infiltrating slander and skin-deep accusations do not operate, one may be called clear-sighted."
translations.idiomatic: Confucius said: "If infiltrating slander and skin-deep accusations do not operate, one may be called clear-sighted."

sentences.zh: 賢佞分別，官人有序，帥由舊章，敬重功勳，殊別適庶，如此則火得其性矣。

translations.literal: If worthy and crooked are distinguished, appointments are orderly, old statutes followed, merit honored, and legitimate and secondary lines distinguished, then fire gets its nature.
translations.idiomatic: If worthy and crooked are distinguished, appointments are orderly, old statutes followed, merit honored, and legitimate and secondary lines distinguished, then fire gets its nature.

sentences.zh: 若乃信道不篤，或燿虛偽，讒夫昌，邪勝正，則火失其性矣。

translations.literal: But if trust in the Way is shallow and one flaunts false glitter, slanderers prosper and crooked overcomes right, then fire loses its nature.
translations.idiomatic: But if trust in the Way is shallow and one flaunts false glitter, slanderers prosper and crooked overcomes right, then fire loses its nature.

sentences.zh: 自上而降，及濫炎妄起，災宗廟，燒宮館，雖興師眾，弗能救也，是為火不炎上。

translations.literal: Then from above downward flames rise recklessly, burning ancestral temples and palace halls; even with mobilized masses they cannot be saved - this is fire not blazing upward.
translations.idiomatic: Then from above downward flames rise recklessly, burning ancestral temples and palace halls; even with mobilized masses they cannot be saved - this is fire not blazing upward.

sentences.zh: 春秋桓公十四年「八月壬申，御廩災」。

translations.literal: Spring and Autumn, Huan year fourteen: "In eighth month ren-shen, the imperial granary burned."
translations.idiomatic: Spring and Autumn, Huan year fourteen: "In eighth month ren-shen, the imperial granary burned."

sentences.zh: 董仲舒以為先是四國共伐魯，大破之於龍門。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu held that before this four states jointly attacked Lu and greatly defeated it at Longmen.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu held that before this four states jointly attacked Lu and greatly defeated it at Longmen.

sentences.zh: 百姓傷者未瘳，怨咎未復，而君臣俱惰，內怠政事，外侮四鄰，非能保守宗廟終其天年者也，故天災御廩以戒之。

translations.literal: The wounded people had not recovered, grievances were unresolved, yet ruler and ministers were lax, neglecting internal governance and insulting neighboring states, no longer able to preserve ancestral temples through heaven-allotted years; therefore Heaven burned the granary as warning.
translations.idiomatic: The wounded people had not recovered, grievances were unresolved, yet ruler and ministers were lax, neglecting internal governance and insulting neighboring states, no longer able to preserve ancestral temples through heaven-allotted years; therefore Heaven burned the granary as warning.

sentences.zh: 劉向以為御廩，夫人八妾所舂米之臧以奉宗廟者也，時夫人有淫行，挾逆心，天戒若曰，夫人不可以奉宗廟。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held that the imperial granary stored rice pounded by the principal wife and eight concubines for ancestral offerings; at that time the wife had illicit conduct and rebellious intent, so Heaven's warning meant she could not serve the ancestral temple.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held that the imperial granary stored rice pounded by the principal wife and eight concubines for ancestral offerings; at that time the wife had illicit conduct and rebellious intent, so Heaven's warning meant she could not serve the ancestral temple.

sentences.zh: 桓不寤，與夫人俱會齊，夫人譖桓公於齊侯，齊侯殺桓公。

translations.literal: Huan did not awaken; he and the wife met Qi; the wife slandered Duke Huan to the marquis of Qi, who killed Duke Huan.
translations.idiomatic: Huan did not awaken; he and the wife met Qi; the wife slandered Duke Huan to the marquis of Qi, who killed Duke Huan.

sentences.zh: 劉歆以為御廩，公所親耕籍田以奉粢盛者也，棄法度亡禮之應也。

translations.literal: Liu Xin held that the granary was where the duke's own plowed ritual fields supplied grain offerings, and this was response to abandoning law and ritual.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin held that the granary was where the duke's own plowed ritual fields supplied grain offerings, and this was response to abandoning law and ritual.

sentences.zh: 嚴公二十年「夏，齊大災」。

translations.literal: Zhuang year twenty: "In summer, great fire in Qi."
translations.idiomatic: Zhuang year twenty: "In summer, great fire in Qi."

sentences.zh: 劉向以為齊桓好色，聽女口，以妾為妻，適庶數更，故致太災。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held that Duke Huan of Qi loved beauty and listened to women's words, making a concubine his wife and repeatedly changing legitimate and secondary lines, thereby bringing great fire.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held that Duke Huan of Qi loved beauty and listened to women's words, making a concubine his wife and repeatedly changing legitimate and secondary lines, thereby bringing great fire.

sentences.zh: 桓公不寤，及死，適庶分爭，九月不得葬。

translations.literal: Duke Huan did not awaken; after his death, legitimate and secondary heirs fought, and he could not be buried for nine months.
translations.idiomatic: Duke Huan did not awaken; after his death, legitimate and secondary heirs fought, and he could not be buried for nine months.

sentences.zh: 公羊傳曰，大災，疫也。

translations.literal: The Gongyang Commentary says great fire means epidemic.
translations.idiomatic: The Gongyang Commentary says great fire means epidemic.

sentences.zh: 董仲舒以為魯夫人淫於齊，齊桓姊妹不嫁者七人。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu held that Lu's wife committed adultery in Qi, and Duke Huan had seven sisters unmarried.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu held that Lu's wife committed adultery in Qi, and Duke Huan had seven sisters unmarried.

sentences.zh: 國君，民之父母；

translations.literal: The ruler is parent of the people.
translations.idiomatic: The ruler is parent of the people.

sentences.zh: 夫婦，生化之本。

translations.literal: Husband and wife are the root of generation.
translations.idiomatic: Husband and wife are the root of generation.

sentences.zh: 本傷則末夭，故天災所予也。

translations.literal: If root is injured, the branches wither; therefore Heaven sent disaster.
translations.idiomatic: If root is injured, the branches wither; therefore Heaven sent disaster.

sentences.zh: 釐公二十年「五月己酉，西宮災」。

translations.literal: Xi year twenty: "On jiyou day of fifth month, fire in West Palace."
translations.idiomatic: Xi year twenty: "On jiyou day of fifth month, fire in West Palace."

sentences.zh: 穀梁以為愍公宮也，以諡言之則若疏，故謂之西宮。

translations.literal: Guliang says it was Min Duke's palace; to call by posthumous title would seem distant, so it was called West Palace.
translations.idiomatic: Guliang says it was Min Duke's palace; to call by posthumous title would seem distant, so it was called West Palace.

sentences.zh: 劉向以為釐立妾母為夫人以入宗廟，故天災愍宮，若曰，去其卑而親者，將害宗廟之正禮。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held that Xi installed his concubine-mother as principal wife and brought her into ancestral temple, so Heaven burned Min palace as if saying: removing the lowly yet close one will harm proper ancestral rites.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held that Xi installed his concubine-mother as principal wife and brought her into ancestral temple, so Heaven burned Min palace as if saying: removing the lowly yet close one will harm proper ancestral rites.

sentences.zh: 董仲舒以為釐娶於楚，而齊媵之，脅公使立以為夫人。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu held Xi married from Chu and received a Qi woman as accompanying bride, who coerced the duke into making her principal wife.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu held Xi married from Chu and received a Qi woman as accompanying bride, who coerced the duke into making her principal wife.

sentences.zh: 西宮者，小寢，夫人之居也。

translations.literal: West Palace is the lesser sleeping quarters, where the wife lived.
translations.idiomatic: West Palace is the lesser sleeping quarters, where the wife lived.

sentences.zh: 若曰，妾何為此宮！

translations.literal: As if saying: what is a concubine doing in this palace?
translations.idiomatic: As if saying: what is a concubine doing in this palace?

sentences.zh: 誅去之意也。

translations.literal: It signified intent to punish and remove her.
translations.idiomatic: It signified intent to punish and remove her.

sentences.zh: 以天災之，故大之曰西宮也。

translations.literal: Heaven therefore sent fire and so it was magnified as "West Palace."
translations.idiomatic: Heaven therefore sent fire and so it was magnified as "West Palace."

sentences.zh: 左氏以為西宮者，公宮也。

translations.literal: The Zuo Tradition holds West Palace was ducal palace.
translations.idiomatic: The Zuo Tradition holds West Palace was ducal palace.

sentences.zh: 言西，知有東。

translations.literal: To say west implies there was east.
translations.idiomatic: To say west implies there was east.

sentences.zh: 東宮，太子所居。

translations.literal: East Palace is where the crown prince resides.
translations.idiomatic: East Palace is where the crown prince resides.

sentences.zh: 言宮，舉區皆災也。

translations.literal: To say palace means the entire district burned.
translations.idiomatic: To say palace means the entire district burned.

sentences.zh: 宣公十六年「夏，成周宣榭火」。

translations.literal: Xuan year sixteen: "In summer, fire at Xuanshe in Chengzhou."
translations.idiomatic: Xuan year sixteen: "In summer, fire at Xuanshe in Chengzhou."

sentences.zh: 榭者，所以臧樂器，宣其名也。

translations.literal: A xie was where musical instruments were stored and proclaimed by name.
translations.idiomatic: A xie was where musical instruments were stored and proclaimed by name.

sentences.zh: 董仲舒、劉向以為十五年王札子殺召伯、毛伯，天子不能誅。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu and Liu Xiang held that in year fifteen Prince Zha had killed Shao Bo and Mao Bo, and the Son of Heaven could not punish him.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu and Liu Xiang held that in year fifteen Prince Zha had killed Shao Bo and Mao Bo, and the Son of Heaven could not punish him.

sentences.zh: 天戒若曰，不能行政令，何以禮樂為而臧之？

translations.literal: Heaven warned: if you cannot carry out government commands, what use are ritual and music to store them?
translations.idiomatic: Heaven warned: if you cannot carry out government commands, what use are ritual and music to store them?

sentences.zh: 左氏經曰：「

translations.literal: The Zuo tradition text says:
translations.idiomatic: The Zuo tradition text says:

sentences.zh: 成周宣榭火，人火也。

translations.literal: "Fire at Xuanshe in Chengzhou was human fire.
translations.idiomatic: "Fire at Xuanshe in Chengzhou was human fire.

sentences.zh: 人火曰火，天火曰災。」

translations.literal: Human fire is called fire; heavenly fire is called calamity."
translations.idiomatic: Human fire is called fire; heavenly fire is called calamity."

sentences.zh: 榭者，講武之坐屋。

translations.literal: A xie was a seated hall for military exercises.
translations.idiomatic: A xie was a seated hall for military exercises.

sentences.zh: 成公三年「二月甲子，新宮災」。

translations.literal: Cheng year three: "On jiazi day of second month, new palace burned."
translations.idiomatic: Cheng year three: "On jiazi day of second month, new palace burned."

sentences.zh: 穀梁以為宣宮，不言諡，恭也。

translations.literal: Guliang says this was Xuan palace; not using posthumous title was an expression of reverence.
translations.idiomatic: Guliang says this was Xuan palace; not using posthumous title was an expression of reverence.

sentences.zh: 劉向以為時魯三桓子孫始執國政，宣公欲誅之，恐不能，使大夫公孫歸父如晉謀。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held that at this time descendants of Lu's three Huan houses had begun to control state affairs; Duke Xuan wanted to execute them and, fearing inability, sent Grandee Gongsun Guifu to Jin for plotting.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held that at this time descendants of Lu's three Huan houses had begun to control state affairs; Duke Xuan wanted to execute them and, fearing inability, sent Grandee Gongsun Guifu to Jin for plotting.

sentences.zh: 未反，宣公死。

translations.literal: Before he returned, Duke Xuan died.
translations.idiomatic: Before he returned, Duke Xuan died.

sentences.zh: 三家譖歸父於成公。

translations.literal: The three houses slandered Guifu before Duke Cheng.
translations.idiomatic: The three houses slandered Guifu before Duke Cheng.

sentences.zh: 成公父喪未葬，聽讒而逐其父之臣，使奔齊，故天災宣宮，明不用父命之象也。

translations.literal: Before his father's burial was complete, Duke Cheng listened to slander and expelled his father's minister, sending him to Qi in exile; thus Heaven burned Xuan's palace, showing the image of not following a father's charge.
translations.idiomatic: Before his father's burial was complete, Duke Cheng listened to slander and expelled his father's minister, sending him to Qi in exile; thus Heaven burned Xuan's palace, showing the image of not following a father's charge.

sentences.zh: 一曰，三家親而亡禮，猶宣公殺子赤而立。

translations.literal: Another view: the three houses were close yet without ritual, just as Duke Xuan killed Zichi and took the throne.
translations.idiomatic: Another view: the three houses were close yet without ritual, just as Duke Xuan killed Zichi and took the throne.

sentences.zh: 亡禮而親，天災宣廟，欲示去三家也。

translations.literal: Being intimate yet without ritual, Heaven burned Xuan's temple to signal removal of the three houses.
translations.idiomatic: Being intimate yet without ritual, Heaven burned Xuan's temple to signal removal of the three houses.

sentences.zh: 董仲舒以為成居喪亡哀戚心，數興兵戰伐，故天災其父廟，示失子道，不能奉宗廟也。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu held that while in mourning, Duke Cheng lacked grief and repeatedly launched military campaigns; therefore Heaven burned his father's temple, showing loss of filial way and inability to serve ancestral shrines.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu held that while in mourning, Duke Cheng lacked grief and repeatedly launched military campaigns; therefore Heaven burned his father's temple, showing loss of filial way and inability to serve ancestral shrines.

sentences.zh: 一曰，宣殺君而立，不當列於群祖也。

translations.literal: Another view: since Xuan killed his ruler and took power, he should not have been listed among ancestral lines.
translations.idiomatic: Another view: since Xuan killed his ruler and took power, he should not have been listed among ancestral lines.

sentences.zh: 襄公九年「春，宋災」。

translations.literal: Xiang year nine: "In spring, fire in Song."
translations.idiomatic: Xiang year nine: "In spring, fire in Song."

sentences.zh: 劉向以為先是宋公聽讒，逐其大夫華弱，出奔魯。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held that before this the Duke of Song listened to slander and expelled his grandee Hua Ruo, who fled to Lu.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held that before this the Duke of Song listened to slander and expelled his grandee Hua Ruo, who fled to Lu.

sentences.zh: 左氏傳曰，宋災，樂喜為司城，先使火所未至徹小屋，塗大屋，陳畚輂，具綆缶，備水器，畜水潦，積土塗，繕守備，表火道，儲正徒。

translations.literal: Zuo Tradition says: at the Song fire, Le Xi as city commander first had small houses removed where fire had not reached, mud-coated large houses, arranged baskets and carts, prepared ropes and jars, readied water vessels, stored floodwater, piled mud for coating, repaired defenses, marked firebreak routes, and mustered laborers.
translations.idiomatic: Zuo Tradition says: at the Song fire, Le Xi as city commander first had small houses removed where fire had not reached, mud-coated large houses, arranged baskets and carts, prepared ropes and jars, readied water vessels, stored floodwater, piled mud for coating, repaired defenses, marked firebreak routes, and mustered laborers.

sentences.zh: 郊保之民，使奔火所。

translations.literal: People assigned to suburban defense were sent to where fire raged.
translations.idiomatic: People assigned to suburban defense were sent to where fire raged.

sentences.zh: 又飭眾官，各慎其職。

translations.literal: He also ordered all offices to be cautious in their duties.
translations.idiomatic: He also ordered all offices to be cautious in their duties.

sentences.zh: 晉侯聞之，問士弱曰：「宋災，於是乎知有天道，何故？」

translations.literal: The marquis of Jin heard this and asked Shi Ruo: "From Song's fire, how does one know there is heavenly order? Why so?"
translations.idiomatic: The marquis of Jin heard this and asked Shi Ruo: "From Song's fire, how does one know there is heavenly order? Why so?"

sentences.zh: 對曰：「古之火正，或食於心，或食於咮，以出入火。

translations.literal: He replied: "Ancient fire-officials fed either at Heart or at Beak and thereby managed the use and stopping of fire.
translations.idiomatic: He replied: "Ancient fire-officials fed either at Heart or at Beak and thereby managed the use and stopping of fire.

sentences.zh: 是故咮為鶉火，心為大火。

translations.literal: Thus Beak is Quail Fire and Heart is Great Fire.
translations.idiomatic: Thus Beak is Quail Fire and Heart is Great Fire.

sentences.zh: 陶唐氏之火正閼伯，居商丘，祀大火，而火紀時焉。

translations.literal: The fire-official of Tao-Tang, Ebo, lived at Shangqiu, sacrificed to Great Fire, and from it fixed fire seasons.
translations.idiomatic: The fire-official of Tao-Tang, Ebo, lived at Shangqiu, sacrificed to Great Fire, and from it fixed fire seasons.

sentences.zh: 相土因之，故商主大火。

translations.literal: Xiangtu followed this; therefore Shang took Great Fire as principal.
translations.idiomatic: Xiangtu followed this; therefore Shang took Great Fire as principal.

sentences.zh: 商人閱其禍敗之釁必始於火，是以知有天道。」

translations.literal: Shang people observed that omens of their disasters and defeats always began from fire; thus they knew heavenly order."
translations.idiomatic: Shang people observed that omens of their disasters and defeats always began from fire; thus they knew heavenly order."

sentences.zh: 公曰：「可必乎？」

translations.literal: The duke asked: "Can this be certain?"
translations.idiomatic: The duke asked: "Can this be certain?"

sentences.zh: 對曰：「在道。

translations.literal: He replied: "It depends on the Way.
translations.idiomatic: He replied: "It depends on the Way.

sentences.zh: 國亂亡象，不可知也。」

translations.literal: If a state is disorderly and lacks signs, it cannot be known."
translations.idiomatic: If a state is disorderly and lacks signs, it cannot be known."

sentences.zh: 說曰：古之火正，謂火官也，掌祭火星，行火政。

translations.literal: Explanation: ancient fire-official means the office of fire, in charge of sacrificing the fire star and executing fire policy.
translations.idiomatic: Explanation: ancient fire-official means the office of fire, in charge of sacrificing the fire star and executing fire policy.

sentences.zh: 季春昏，心星出東方，而咮、七星、鳥首正在南方，則用火；

translations.literal: In late spring at dusk, when Heart rises in east and Beak, Qixing, and bird-head are due south, fire is used.
translations.idiomatic: In late spring at dusk, when Heart rises in east and Beak, Qixing, and bird-head are due south, fire is used.

sentences.zh: 季秋，星入，則止火，以順天時，救民疾。

translations.literal: In late autumn when the star enters, fire is stopped, following Heaven's timing and relieving people's suffering.
translations.idiomatic: In late autumn when the star enters, fire is stopped, following Heaven's timing and relieving people's suffering.

sentences.zh: 帝嚳則有祝融，堯時有閼伯，民賴其德，死則以為火祖，配祭火星，故曰「或食於心，或食於咮也」。

translations.literal: Under Emperor Ku there was Zhurong; in Yao's time there was Ebo. The people relied on their virtue; after death they became fire ancestors and were paired in sacrifice with fire stars. Hence "fed at Heart" or "fed at Beak."
translations.idiomatic: Under Emperor Ku there was Zhurong; in Yao's time there was Ebo. The people relied on their virtue; after death they became fire ancestors and were paired in sacrifice with fire stars. Hence "fed at Heart" or "fed at Beak."

sentences.zh: 相土，商祖契之曾孫，代閼伯後主火星。

translations.literal: Xiangtu, great-grandson of Qi the Shang ancestor, succeeded Ebo in governing fire stars.
translations.idiomatic: Xiangtu, great-grandson of Qi the Shang ancestor, succeeded Ebo in governing fire stars.

sentences.zh: 宋，其後也。

translations.literal: Song was his later line.
translations.idiomatic: Song was his later line.

sentences.zh: 世司其占，故先知火災。

translations.literal: For generations they managed this divination, so they knew fire calamity in advance.
translations.idiomatic: For generations they managed this divination, so they knew fire calamity in advance.

sentences.zh: 賢君見變，能修道以除凶；

translations.literal: When worthy rulers see anomalies, they can cultivate the Way and remove inauspice.
translations.idiomatic: When worthy rulers see anomalies, they can cultivate the Way and remove inauspice.

sentences.zh: 亂君亡象，天不譴告，故不可必也。

translations.literal: Where rulers are disorderly and signs absent, Heaven gives no warning, so certainty is impossible.
translations.idiomatic: Where rulers are disorderly and signs absent, Heaven gives no warning, so certainty is impossible.

sentences.zh: 三十年「五月甲午，宋災」。

translations.literal: Year thirty: "On jiawu day of fifth month, fire in Song."
translations.idiomatic: Year thirty: "On jiawu day of fifth month, fire in Song."

sentences.zh: 董仲舒以為伯姬如宋五年，宋恭公卒，伯姬幽居守節三十餘年，又憂傷國家之患禍，積陰生陽，故火生災也。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu held that after Bo Ji married into Song five years, Duke Gong of Song died; Bo Ji lived secluded and chaste for over thirty years, then with accumulated sorrow over state calamities, extreme yin generated yang, and thus fire disaster arose.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu held that after Bo Ji married into Song five years, Duke Gong of Song died; Bo Ji lived secluded and chaste for over thirty years, then with accumulated sorrow over state calamities, extreme yin generated yang, and thus fire disaster arose.

sentences.zh: 劉向以為先是宋公聽讒而殺太子痤，應火不炎上之罰也。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held that before this the Duke of Song listened to slander and killed crown prince Zuo, corresponding to punishment for fire not blazing upward.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held that before this the Duke of Song listened to slander and killed crown prince Zuo, corresponding to punishment for fire not blazing upward.

sentences.zh: 左氏傳昭公六年「六月丙戌，鄭災」。

translations.literal: Zhao year six in Zuo Tradition: "On bingxu day of sixth month, fire in Zheng."
translations.idiomatic: Zhao year six in Zuo Tradition: "On bingxu day of sixth month, fire in Zheng."

sentences.zh: 是春三月，鄭人鑄刑書。

translations.literal: That spring in third month, Zheng cast penal statutes in bronze.
translations.idiomatic: That spring in third month, Zheng cast penal statutes in bronze.

sentences.zh: 士文伯曰：「火見，鄭其火乎？

translations.literal: Shi Wenbo said: "Fire star appears - is Zheng about to burn?
translations.idiomatic: Shi Wenbo said: "Fire star appears - is Zheng about to burn?

sentences.zh: 火未出而作火以鑄刑器，臧爭辟焉。

translations.literal: Before fire-star emergence, they used fire to cast penal instruments and stored contentious punishments therein.
translations.idiomatic: Before fire-star emergence, they used fire to cast penal instruments and stored contentious punishments therein.

sentences.zh: 火而象之，不火何為？」

translations.literal: If they model by fire, what if fire does not burn?"
translations.idiomatic: If they model by fire, what if fire does not burn?"

sentences.zh: 說曰：火星出於周五月，而鄭以三月作火鑄鼎，刻刑辟書，以為民約，是為刑器爭辟。

translations.literal: Explanation: the fire star appears in Zhou fifth month, but Zheng in third month used fire to cast cauldrons and engrave penal texts as contracts for the people - this is contentious penal instrumentation.
translations.idiomatic: Explanation: the fire star appears in Zhou fifth month, but Zheng in third month used fire to cast cauldrons and engrave penal texts as contracts for the people - this is contentious penal instrumentation.

sentences.zh: 故火星出，與五行之火爭明為災，其象然也，又棄法律之占也。

translations.literal: Therefore when fire star appeared it contended in brightness with the elemental fire and became disaster; the image is thus, and it is also an omen of abandoning legal norms.
translations.idiomatic: Therefore when fire star appeared it contended in brightness with the elemental fire and became disaster; the image is thus, and it is also an omen of abandoning legal norms.

sentences.zh: 不書於經，時不告魯也。

translations.literal: It was not recorded in the Classic because Zheng did not report to Lu at the time.
translations.idiomatic: It was not recorded in the Classic because Zheng did not report to Lu at the time.

sentences.zh: 九年「夏四月，陳火」。

translations.literal: Year nine: "In summer fourth month, fire in Chen."
translations.idiomatic: Year nine: "In summer fourth month, fire in Chen."

sentences.zh: 董仲舒以為陳夏徵舒殺君，楚嚴王託欲為陳討賊，陳國闢門而待之，至因滅陳。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu held that in Chen, Xia Zhengshu killed his ruler; King Zhuang of Chu pretended to punish Chen's traitor, and Chen opened its gates to receive him, whereupon Chu destroyed Chen.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu held that in Chen, Xia Zhengshu killed his ruler; King Zhuang of Chu pretended to punish Chen's traitor, and Chen opened its gates to receive him, whereupon Chu destroyed Chen.

sentences.zh: 陳臣子尤毒恨甚，極陰生陽，故致火災。

translations.literal: Chen's ministers and sons had deep hatred and poison, and extreme yin generated yang, thus bringing fire calamity.
translations.idiomatic: Chen's ministers and sons had deep hatred and poison, and extreme yin generated yang, thus bringing fire calamity.

sentences.zh: 劉向以為先是陳侯弟招殺陳太子偃師，皆外事，不因其宮館者，略之也。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held that before this, Chen ruler's younger brother Zhao had killed crown prince Yanshi; as these were outside matters not in palace halls, they were omitted.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held that before this, Chen ruler's younger brother Zhao had killed crown prince Yanshi; as these were outside matters not in palace halls, they were omitted.

sentences.zh: 八年十月壬午，楚師滅陳，春秋不與蠻夷滅中國，故復書陳火也。

translations.literal: On renwu day of eighth year's tenth month, Chu forces destroyed Chen; Spring and Autumn does not approve barbarians destroying central states, therefore it again recorded Chen fire.
translations.idiomatic: On renwu day of eighth year's tenth month, Chu forces destroyed Chen; Spring and Autumn does not approve barbarians destroying central states, therefore it again recorded Chen fire.

sentences.zh: 左氏經曰「陳災」。

translations.literal: The Zuo text records simply "Chen calamity."
translations.idiomatic: The Zuo text records simply "Chen calamity."

sentences.zh: 傳曰「鄭屓灶曰：『五年，陳將復封，封五十二年而遂亡。』

translations.literal: The Tradition says: Zheng diviner Zao said, "In five years Chen will be re-enfeoffed, and after fifty-two years it will finally perish."
translations.idiomatic: The Tradition says: Zheng diviner Zao said, "In five years Chen will be re-enfeoffed, and after fifty-two years it will finally perish."

sentences.zh: 子產問其故，對曰：『陳，水屬也。

translations.literal: Zichan asked why.
translations.idiomatic: Zichan asked why.

sentences.zh: 火，水妃也，而楚所相也。

translations.literal: He replied: "Chen belongs to water.
translations.idiomatic: He replied: "Chen belongs to water.

sentences.zh: 今火出而火陳，逐楚而建陳也。

translations.literal: Fire is water's consort, and Chu is aligned with fire.
translations.idiomatic: Fire is water's consort, and Chu is aligned with fire.

sentences.zh: 妃以五陳，故曰五年。

translations.literal: Now fire appears and burns Chen, expelling Chu and reestablishing Chen.
translations.idiomatic: Now fire appears and burns Chen, expelling Chu and reestablishing Chen.

sentences.zh: 歲五及鶉火，而後陳卒亡，楚克有之，天之道也。』」

translations.literal: By consort-five completion, thus five years.
translations.idiomatic: By consort-five completion, thus five years.

sentences.zh: 說曰：顓頊以水王，陳其族也。

translations.literal: Explanation says: Zhuanxu ruled by water, and Chen was his lineage.
translations.idiomatic: Explanation says: Zhuanxu ruled by water, and Chen was his lineage.

sentences.zh: 今茲歲在星紀，後五年在大梁。

translations.literal: This year is in Xingji, and five years later in Daliang.
translations.idiomatic: This year is in Xingji, and five years later in Daliang.

sentences.zh: 大梁，昴也。

translations.literal: Daliang corresponds to Mao.
translations.idiomatic: Daliang corresponds to Mao.

sentences.zh: 金為水宗，得其宗而昌，故曰「五年陳將復封」。

translations.literal: Metal is parent of water; obtaining its parent brings prosperity, hence "in five years Chen will be re-enfeoffed."
translations.idiomatic: Metal is parent of water; obtaining its parent brings prosperity, hence "in five years Chen will be re-enfeoffed."

sentences.zh: 楚之先為火正，故曰「楚所相也」。

translations.literal: Chu's ancestors were fire officials; therefore "Chu is what it aligns with."
translations.idiomatic: Chu's ancestors were fire officials; therefore "Chu is what it aligns with."

sentences.zh: 天以一生水，地以二生火，天以三生木，地以四生金，天以五生土。

translations.literal: Heaven by one generates water; Earth by two generates fire; Heaven by three generates wood; Earth by four generates metal; Heaven by five generates earth.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven by one generates water; Earth by two generates fire; Heaven by three generates wood; Earth by four generates metal; Heaven by five generates earth.

sentences.zh: 五位皆以五而合，而陰陽易位，故曰「妃以五成」。

translations.literal: All five positions combine by five, and yin-yang exchange places; therefore "consort is completed by five."
translations.idiomatic: All five positions combine by five, and yin-yang exchange places; therefore "consort is completed by five."

sentences.zh: 然則水之大數六，火七，木八，金九，土十。

translations.literal: Thus great numbers are: water six, fire seven, wood eight, metal nine, earth ten.
translations.idiomatic: Thus great numbers are: water six, fire seven, wood eight, metal nine, earth ten.

sentences.zh: 故水以天一為火二牡，木以天三為土十牡，土以天五為水六牡，火以天七為金四牡，金以天九為木八牡。

translations.literal: So water takes Heaven one with fire two as male pair; wood takes Heaven three with earth ten as male pair; earth takes Heaven five with water six as male pair; fire takes Heaven seven with metal four as male pair; metal takes Heaven nine with wood eight as male pair.
translations.idiomatic: So water takes Heaven one with fire two as male pair; wood takes Heaven three with earth ten as male pair; earth takes Heaven five with water six as male pair; fire takes Heaven seven with metal four as male pair; metal takes Heaven nine with wood eight as male pair.

sentences.zh: 陽奇為牡，陰耦為妃。

translations.literal: Odd yang is male; even yin is female-consort.
translations.idiomatic: Odd yang is male; even yin is female-consort.

sentences.zh: 故曰「水，火之牡也；

translations.literal: Hence "water is fire's male;
translations.idiomatic: Hence "water is fire's male;

sentences.zh: 火，水妃也」。

translations.literal: fire is water's consort."
translations.idiomatic: fire is water's consort."

sentences.zh: 於易，坎為水，為中男，離為火，為中女，蓋取諸此也。

translations.literal: In the Changes, Kan is water and middle son; Li is fire and middle daughter - this is likely what is taken.
translations.idiomatic: In the Changes, Kan is water and middle son; Li is fire and middle daughter - this is likely what is taken.

sentences.zh: 自大梁四歲而及鶉火，四周四十八歲，凡五及鶉火，五十二年而陳卒亡。

translations.literal: From Daliang four years to Quail Fire, four circuits make forty-eight years; reaching Quail Fire five times gives fifty-two years, then Chen finally perished.
translations.idiomatic: From Daliang four years to Quail Fire, four circuits make forty-eight years; reaching Quail Fire five times gives fifty-two years, then Chen finally perished.

sentences.zh: 火盛水衰，故曰「天之道也」。

translations.literal: Fire prospered and water declined; therefore "this is Heaven's way."
translations.idiomatic: Fire prospered and water declined; therefore "this is Heaven's way."

sentences.zh: 哀公十七年七月己卯，楚滅陳。

translations.literal: In Ai year seventeen, seventh month jimao day, Chu destroyed Chen.
translations.idiomatic: In Ai year seventeen, seventh month jimao day, Chu destroyed Chen.

sentences.zh: 昭十八年「五月壬午，宋、衛、陳、鄭災」。

translations.literal: Zhao year eighteen: "On renwu day of fifth month, fires in Song, Wei, Chen, and Zheng."
translations.idiomatic: Zhao year eighteen: "On renwu day of fifth month, fires in Song, Wei, Chen, and Zheng."

sentences.zh: 董仲舒以為象王室將亂，天下莫救，故災四國，言亡四方也。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu held this symbolized impending disorder in royal house, with none in the realm able to save it; thus four states burned, signifying collapse in four directions.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu held this symbolized impending disorder in royal house, with none in the realm able to save it; thus four states burned, signifying collapse in four directions.

sentences.zh: 又宋、衛、陳、鄭之君皆荒淫於樂，不恤國政，與周室同行。

translations.literal: Also, rulers of Song, Wei, Chen, and Zheng were all licentious in music, neglecting state governance, acting in parallel with the Zhou court.
translations.idiomatic: Also, rulers of Song, Wei, Chen, and Zheng were all licentious in music, neglecting state governance, acting in parallel with the Zhou court.

sentences.zh: 陽失節則火災出，是以同日災也。

translations.literal: When yang loses season, fire calamity appears; therefore they burned on the same day.
translations.idiomatic: When yang loses season, fire calamity appears; therefore they burned on the same day.

sentences.zh: 劉向以為宋、陳，王者之後，衛、鄭，周同姓也。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held Song and Chen were descendants of kings, while Wei and Zheng were Zhou same-surname houses.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held Song and Chen were descendants of kings, while Wei and Zheng were Zhou same-surname houses.

sentences.zh: 時周景王老，劉子、單子事王子猛，尹氏、召伯、毛伯事王子晁。

translations.literal: At that time King Jing of Zhou was old; Liuzi and Danzi served Prince Meng, while Yin clan, Shao Bo, and Mao Bo served Prince Chao.
translations.idiomatic: At that time King Jing of Zhou was old; Liuzi and Danzi served Prince Meng, while Yin clan, Shao Bo, and Mao Bo served Prince Chao.

sentences.zh: 子晁，楚之出也。

translations.literal: Prince Chao was from Chu line.
translations.idiomatic: Prince Chao was from Chu line.

sentences.zh: 及宋、衛、陳、鄭亦皆外附於楚，亡尊周室之心。

translations.literal: Song, Wei, Chen, and Zheng all externally attached to Chu as well, lacking respect for Zhou house.
translations.idiomatic: Song, Wei, Chen, and Zheng all externally attached to Chu as well, lacking respect for Zhou house.

sentences.zh: 後三年，景王崩，王室亂，故天災四國。

translations.literal: Three years later King Jing died and royal house fell into disorder, thus Heaven burned four states.
translations.idiomatic: Three years later King Jing died and royal house fell into disorder, thus Heaven burned four states.

sentences.zh: 天戒若曰，不救周，反從楚，廢世子，立不正，以害王室，明同罪也。

translations.literal: Heaven's warning was: instead of saving Zhou, they followed Chu, deposed rightful heir and set up the improper, harming royal house - clearly shared guilt.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven's warning was: instead of saving Zhou, they followed Chu, deposed rightful heir and set up the improper, harming royal house - clearly shared guilt.

sentences.zh: 定公二年「五月，雉門及兩觀災」。

translations.literal: Ding year two: "In fifth month, fire at Zhi gate and two watchtowers."
translations.idiomatic: Ding year two: "In fifth month, fire at Zhi gate and two watchtowers."

sentences.zh: 董仲舒、劉向以為此皆奢僭過度者也。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu and Liu Xiang held these were all cases of excessive luxury and usurpation.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu and Liu Xiang held these were all cases of excessive luxury and usurpation.

sentences.zh: 先是，季氏逐昭公，昭公死于外。

translations.literal: Before this, the Ji clan expelled Duke Zhao, who died abroad.
translations.idiomatic: Before this, the Ji clan expelled Duke Zhao, who died abroad.

sentences.zh: 定公即位，既不能誅季氏，又用其邪說，淫於女樂，而退孔子。

translations.literal: When Duke Ding took the throne, he could not punish Ji clan, used their crooked counsel, indulged in female music, and dismissed Confucius.
translations.idiomatic: When Duke Ding took the throne, he could not punish Ji clan, used their crooked counsel, indulged in female music, and dismissed Confucius.

sentences.zh: 天戒若曰，去高顯而奢僭者。

translations.literal: Heaven's warning was: remove those high and prominent yet luxurious and usurping.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven's warning was: remove those high and prominent yet luxurious and usurping.

sentences.zh: 一曰，門闕，號令所由出也，今舍大聖而縱有罪，亡以出號令矣。

translations.literal: Another view: gates and towers are where commands issue; now by abandoning the great sage and indulging the guilty, there is no proper issuing of commands.
translations.idiomatic: Another view: gates and towers are where commands issue; now by abandoning the great sage and indulging the guilty, there is no proper issuing of commands.

sentences.zh: 京房易傳曰：「君不思道，厥妖火燒宮。」

translations.literal: Jing Fang's Yijing transmission says: "If ruler does not reflect on the Way, his anomaly is fire burning palaces."
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang's Yijing transmission says: "If ruler does not reflect on the Way, his anomaly is fire burning palaces."

sentences.zh: 哀公三年「五月辛卯，桓、釐宮災」。

translations.literal: Ai year three: "On xinmao day of fifth month, fire at temples of Huan and Xi."
translations.idiomatic: Ai year three: "On xinmao day of fifth month, fire at temples of Huan and Xi."

sentences.zh: 董仲舒、劉向以為此二宮不當立，違禮者也。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu and Liu Xiang held these two temples should not have been established, being against ritual.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu and Liu Xiang held these two temples should not have been established, being against ritual.

sentences.zh: 哀公又以季氏之故不用孔子。

translations.literal: Duke Ai also, because of the Ji clan, did not employ Confucius.
translations.idiomatic: Duke Ai also, because of the Ji clan, did not employ Confucius.

sentences.zh: 孔子在陳聞魯災，曰：「其桓、釐之宮乎！」

translations.literal: When Confucius in Chen heard of Lu's fire, he said: "Was it not Huan and Xi temples?"
translations.idiomatic: When Confucius in Chen heard of Lu's fire, he said: "Was it not Huan and Xi temples?"

sentences.zh: 以為桓，季氏之所出，釐，使季氏世卿者也。

translations.literal: Meaning Huan was source line of Ji clan, and Xi was what made Ji line hereditary grandees.
translations.idiomatic: Meaning Huan was source line of Ji clan, and Xi was what made Ji line hereditary grandees.

sentences.zh: 四年「六月辛丑，亳社災」。

translations.literal: Year four: "On xinchou day of sixth month, fire at Bo altar."
translations.idiomatic: Year four: "On xinchou day of sixth month, fire at Bo altar."

sentences.zh: 董仲舒、劉向以為亡國之社，所以為戒也。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu and Liu Xiang held that altars of destroyed states exist as warnings.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu and Liu Xiang held that altars of destroyed states exist as warnings.

sentences.zh: 天戒若曰，國將危亡，不用戒矣。

translations.literal: Heaven's warning was: the state is about to perish and no warning is being heeded.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven's warning was: the state is about to perish and no warning is being heeded.

sentences.zh: 春秋火災，屢於定、哀之間，不用聖人而縱驕臣，將以亡國，不明甚也。

translations.literal: In Spring and Autumn, fire calamities repeatedly occurred between Ding and Ai; they did not employ the sage and indulged arrogant ministers, and would perish - how clear this is.
translations.idiomatic: In Spring and Autumn, fire calamities repeatedly occurred between Ding and Ai; they did not employ the sage and indulged arrogant ministers, and would perish - how clear this is.

sentences.zh: 一曰，天生孔子，非為定、哀也，蓋失禮不明，火災應之，自然象也。

translations.literal: Another view: Heaven gave Confucius not for Ding and Ai specifically; rather because ritual was lost and clarity absent, fire calamity responded naturally as image.
translations.idiomatic: Another view: Heaven gave Confucius not for Ding and Ai specifically; rather because ritual was lost and clarity absent, fire calamity responded naturally as image.

sentences.zh: 高后元年五月丙申，趙叢臺災。

translations.literal: In Empress Gao year one, on bingshen day of fifth month, fire at Zhao's Congtai.
translations.idiomatic: In Empress Gao year one, on bingshen day of fifth month, fire at Zhao's Congtai.

sentences.zh: 劉向以為是時呂氏女為趙王后，嫉妒，將為讒口以害趙王。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held that at this time a Lü-clan woman was queen of Zhao king and, jealous, was about to use slander to harm the king.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held that at this time a Lü-clan woman was queen of Zhao king and, jealous, was about to use slander to harm the king.

sentences.zh: 王不寤焉，卒見幽殺。

translations.literal: The king did not awaken, and eventually was secluded and killed.
translations.idiomatic: The king did not awaken, and eventually was secluded and killed.

sentences.zh: 景帝中五年八月己酉，未央宮東闕災。

translations.literal: In Emperor Jing middle year five, eighth month jiyou day, fire at east tower of Weiyang Palace.
translations.idiomatic: In Emperor Jing middle year five, eighth month jiyou day, fire at east tower of Weiyang Palace.

sentences.zh: 先是，栗太子廢為臨江王，以罪徵詣中尉，自殺。

translations.literal: Before this, Crown Prince Li was deposed as King of Linjiang, summoned on charges to the commandant, and killed himself.
translations.idiomatic: Before this, Crown Prince Li was deposed as King of Linjiang, summoned on charges to the commandant, and killed himself.

sentences.zh: 丞相條侯周亞夫以不合旨稱疾免，後二年下獄死。

translations.literal: Chancellor Zhou Yafu, Marquis of Tiao, was dismissed for illness because he disagreed with imperial intent, and two years later was imprisoned and died.
translations.idiomatic: Chancellor Zhou Yafu, Marquis of Tiao, was dismissed for illness because he disagreed with imperial intent, and two years later was imprisoned and died.

sentences.zh: 武帝建元六年六月丁酉，遼東高廟災。

translations.literal: In Emperor Wu Jianyuan year six, on dingyou day of sixth month, fire struck the Gao ancestral temple in Liaodong.
translations.idiomatic: In Emperor Wu Jianyuan year six, on dingyou day of sixth month, fire struck the Gao ancestral temple in Liaodong.

sentences.zh: 四月壬子，高園便殿火。

translations.literal: On renzi day of fourth month, a side hall in Gao garden burned.
translations.idiomatic: On renzi day of fourth month, a side hall in Gao garden burned.

sentences.zh: 董仲舒對曰：「春秋之道舉往以明來，是故天下有物，視春秋所舉與同比者，精微眇以存其意，通倫類以貫其理，天地之變，國家之事，粲然皆見，亡所疑矣。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu replied: "The Spring and Autumn way uses the past to illuminate the coming. Therefore when anything appears in the realm, compare it with what Spring and Autumn records as analogous; preserve its subtle intent, connect categories to penetrate principle, and then changes of Heaven and affairs of state are all clearly seen without doubt.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu replied: "The Spring and Autumn way uses the past to illuminate the coming. Therefore when anything appears in the realm, compare it with what Spring and Autumn records as analogous; preserve its subtle intent, connect categories to penetrate principle, and then changes of Heaven and affairs of state are all clearly seen without doubt.

sentences.zh: 按春秋魯定公、哀公時，季氏之惡已孰，而孔子之聖方盛。

translations.literal: Examining the times of Duke Ding and Duke Ai of Lu, the evil of the Ji clan was already mature while the sagehood of Confucius was at its height.
translations.idiomatic: Examining the times of Duke Ding and Duke Ai of Lu, the evil of the Ji clan was already mature while the sagehood of Confucius was at its height.

sentences.zh: 夫以盛聖而易孰惡，季孫雖重，魯君雖輕，其勢可成也。

translations.literal: With sage strength and ripe evil, though Jisun was heavy and Lu ruler light, the trend could have been reversed.
translations.idiomatic: With sage strength and ripe evil, though Jisun was heavy and Lu ruler light, the trend could have been reversed.

sentences.zh: 故定公二年五月兩觀災。

translations.literal: Thus in Duke Ding year two, in fifth month, the two watchtowers burned.
translations.idiomatic: Thus in Duke Ding year two, in fifth month, the two watchtowers burned.

sentences.zh: 兩觀，僭禮之物，天災之者，若曰，僭禮之臣可以去。

translations.literal: The two watchtowers were objects of ritual usurpation; Heaven burned them as if saying that ministers who usurp ritual may be removed.
translations.idiomatic: The two watchtowers were objects of ritual usurpation; Heaven burned them as if saying that ministers who usurp ritual may be removed.

sentences.zh: 已見罪徵，而後告可去，此天意也。

translations.literal: After first showing fault-sign, it then declared removable - this was Heaven's intent.
translations.idiomatic: After showing an omen of guilt, it then declared that the cause could be removed; this was Heaven's will.

sentences.zh: 定公不知省。

translations.literal: Duke Ding did not reflect.
translations.idiomatic: Duke Ding did not reflect.

sentences.zh: 至哀公三年五月，桓宮、釐宮災。

translations.literal: By Duke Ai year three, in fifth month, temples of Huan and Xi burned.
translations.idiomatic: By Duke Ai year three, in fifth month, temples of Huan and Xi burned.

sentences.zh: 二者同事，所為一也，若曰燔貴而去不義云爾。

translations.literal: These two had one cause and one act, as if saying: burn what is esteemed and remove what is unrighteous.
translations.idiomatic: These two were one in cause and one in deed, as if to say: burn what is honored and cast out what is unjust.

sentences.zh: 哀公未能見，故四年六月亳社災。

translations.literal: Duke Ai still could not perceive, so in year four, sixth month, Bo altar burned.
translations.idiomatic: Duke Ai still could not perceive, so in year four, sixth month, Bo altar burned.

sentences.zh: 兩觀、桓、釐廟、亳社，四者皆不當立，天皆燔其不當立者以示魯，欲其去亂臣而用聖人也。

translations.literal: The two watchtowers, Huan and Xi temples, and Bo altar - all four should not have stood; Heaven burned what should not stand to show Lu, intending it to remove disorderly ministers and employ the sage.
translations.idiomatic: The two watchtowers, Huan and Xi temples, and Bo altar - all four should not have stood; Heaven burned what should not stand to show Lu, intending it to remove disorderly ministers and employ the sage.

sentences.zh: 季氏亡道久矣，前是天不見災者，魯未有賢聖臣，雖欲去季孫，其力不能，昭公是也。

translations.literal: The Ji clan had long lacked the Way; before this Heaven showed no disasters because Lu had no worthy sage minister, and though wishing to remove Jisun, lacked strength - as in Duke Zhao's case.
translations.idiomatic: The Ji clan had long lacked the Way; before this Heaven showed no disasters because Lu had no worthy sage minister, and though wishing to remove Jisun, lacked strength - as in Duke Zhao's case.

sentences.zh: 至定、哀乃見之，其時可也。

translations.literal: By Ding and Ai the signs appeared, because the time had become possible.
translations.idiomatic: By Ding and Ai the signs appeared, because the time had become possible.

sentences.zh: 不時不見，天之道也。

translations.literal: If not timely, signs do not appear: this is Heaven's way.
translations.idiomatic: If not timely, signs do not appear: this is Heaven's way.

sentences.zh: 今高廟不當居遼東，高園殿不當居陵旁，於禮亦不當立，與魯所災同。

translations.literal: Now the Gao temple should not be in Liaodong, and Gao garden hall should not be beside the mausoleum; ritually they too should not stand, same as those Lu disasters.
translations.idiomatic: Now the Gao temple should not be in Liaodong, and Gao garden hall should not be beside the mausoleum; ritually they too should not stand, same as those Lu disasters.

sentences.zh: 其不當立久矣，至於陛下時天乃災之者，殆亦其時可也。

translations.literal: They had long been unfit; that Heaven only now sent disaster in Your Majesty's time likely means the time has become possible.
translations.idiomatic: They had long been unfit; that Heaven only now sent disaster in Your Majesty's time likely means the time has become possible.

sentences.zh: 昔秦受亡周之敝，而亡以化之；

translations.literal: Formerly Qin inherited the corruption of fallen Zhou yet did not transform it.
translations.idiomatic: Formerly Qin inherited the corruption of fallen Zhou yet did not transform it.

sentences.zh: 漢受亡秦之敝，又亡以化之。

translations.literal: Han inherited corruption of fallen Qin and also did not transform it.
translations.idiomatic: Han inherited corruption of fallen Qin and also did not transform it.

sentences.zh: 夫繼二敝之後，承其下流，兼受其猥，難治甚矣。

translations.literal: To succeed two corruptions and inherit their lower stream while receiving their coarseness is extremely hard to govern.
translations.idiomatic: To inherit the aftermath of two eras of decay, taking on their degraded currents and their coarseness, is exceedingly hard to govern.

sentences.zh: 又多兄弟親戚骨肉之連，驕揚奢侈恣睢者眾，所謂重難之時者也。

translations.literal: Further, many brothers, kin, and blood ties produce crowds who are arrogant, extravagant, and unruly; this is what is called a doubly difficult age.
translations.idiomatic: Further, many brothers, kin, and blood ties produce crowds who are arrogant, extravagant, and unruly; this is what is called a doubly difficult age.

sentences.zh: 陛下正當大敝之後，又遭重難之時，甚可憂也。

translations.literal: Your Majesty is precisely after great corruption and in doubly difficult times; this is deeply worrisome.
translations.idiomatic: Your Majesty now stands in the wake of great decay and at a time of compounded hardship; this is deeply troubling.

sentences.zh: 故天災若語陛下：『當今之世，雖敝而重難，非以太平至公，不能治也。

translations.literal: Therefore Heaven's disasters speak to Your Majesty: 'In this age, though corrupt and difficult, unless governed by utmost fairness and great impartiality it cannot be ruled.
translations.idiomatic: So Heaven's disasters seem to tell Your Majesty: 'In this present age, though decayed and burdened by hardship, it cannot be governed unless with supreme fairness and utmost impartiality.'

sentences.zh: 視親戚貴屬在諸侯遠正最甚者，忍而誅之，如吾燔遼高廟乃可；

translations.literal: Regarding kin and noble relatives among feudal houses who are most remote from rectitude, endure and execute them, as I burned the Liaodong Gao temple.
translations.idiomatic: Regarding kin and noble relatives among feudal houses who are most remote from rectitude, endure and execute them, as I burned the Liaodong Gao temple.

sentences.zh: 視近臣在國中處旁仄及貴而不正者，忍而誅之，如吾燔高園殿乃可』云爾。

translations.literal: Regarding close ministers in the state who hold sidelined positions yet are noble and unrectified, endure and execute them, as I burned the Gao garden hall.'
translations.idiomatic: Regarding close ministers in the state who hold sidelined positions yet are noble and unrectified, endure and execute them, as I burned the Gao garden hall.'

sentences.zh: 在外而不正者，雖貴如高廟，猶災燔之，況諸侯乎！

translations.literal: If those outside and unrectified are burned by Heaven though as noble as the Gao temple, how much more feudal lords!
translations.idiomatic: If those outside and unrectified are burned by Heaven though as noble as the Gao temple, how much more feudal lords!

sentences.zh: 在內不正者，雖貴如高園殿，猶燔災之，況大臣乎！

translations.literal: If those inside and unrectified are burned by Heaven though as noble as the Gao garden hall, how much more great ministers!
translations.idiomatic: If those inside and unrectified are burned by Heaven though as noble as the Gao garden hall, how much more great ministers!

sentences.zh: 此天意也。

translations.literal: This is Heaven's intention.
translations.idiomatic: This is Heaven's intention.

sentences.zh: 罪在外者天災外，罪在內者天災內，燔甚罪當重，燔簡罪當輕，承天意之道也。」

translations.literal: If guilt is outside, Heaven sends outside calamity; if guilt is inside, Heaven sends inside calamity. Severe burning means heavy guilt, light burning means lighter guilt - this is the way of receiving Heaven's intent."
translations.idiomatic: If guilt is outside, Heaven sends outside calamity; if guilt is inside, Heaven sends inside calamity. Severe burning means heavy guilt, light burning means lighter guilt - this is the way of receiving Heaven's intent."

sentences.zh: 先是，淮南王安入朝，始與帝舅太尉武安侯田蚡有逆言。

translations.literal: Before this, King An of Huainan came to court and first exchanged rebellious words with imperial uncle, Grand Commandant Marquis of Wu'an Tian Fen.
translations.idiomatic: Before this, King An of Huainan came to court and first exchanged rebellious words with imperial uncle, Grand Commandant Marquis of Wu'an Tian Fen.

sentences.zh: 其後膠西于王、趙敬肅王、常山憲王皆數犯法，或至夷滅人家，藥殺二千石，而淮南、衡山王遂謀反。

translations.literal: Later kings of Jiaoxi, Zhao Jingsu, and Changshan Xian repeatedly broke law, some exterminating households and poisoning two-thousand-bushel officials; then kings of Huainan and Hengshan plotted rebellion.
translations.idiomatic: After that, the kings of Jiaoxi, Zhao Jingsu, and Changshan Xian repeatedly violated the law; some even exterminated households and poisoned officials of two-thousand-bushel rank, and then the kings of Huainan and Hengshan plotted rebellion.

sentences.zh: 膠東、江都王皆知其謀，陰治兵弩，欲以應之。

translations.literal: Kings of Jiaodong and Jiangdu both knew the plot, secretly preparing troops and crossbows to respond.
translations.idiomatic: Kings of Jiaodong and Jiangdu both knew the plot, secretly preparing troops and crossbows to respond.

sentences.zh: 至元朔六年，乃發覺而伏辜。

translations.literal: By Yuanshuo year six it was exposed and they were punished.
translations.idiomatic: By Yuanshuo year six it was exposed and they were punished.

sentences.zh: 時田蚡已死，不及誅。

translations.literal: At that time Tian Fen had already died and could not be executed.
translations.idiomatic: At that time Tian Fen had already died and could not be executed.

sentences.zh: 上思仲舒前言，使仲舒弟子呂步舒持斧鉞治淮南獄，以春秋誼顓斷於外，不請。

translations.literal: The emperor recalled Zhongshu's earlier words and had Zhongshu's disciple Lü Bushu carry axe and halberd to try the Huainan case, deciding outside by Spring and Autumn principle without requesting further approval.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor recalled Zhongshu's earlier words and had Zhongshu's disciple Lü Bushu carry axe and halberd to try the Huainan case, deciding outside by Spring and Autumn principle without requesting further approval.

sentences.zh: 既還奏事，上皆是之。

translations.literal: After he returned and memorialized, the emperor approved everything.
translations.idiomatic: After he returned and memorialized, the emperor approved everything.

sentences.zh: 征和二年春，涿郡鐵官鑄鐵，鐵銷，皆飛上去，此火為變使之然也。

translations.literal: In Zhenghe year two spring, at Zhuo commandery iron office during casting, molten iron flew upward and away; this happened because fire transformed it.
translations.idiomatic: In the spring of Zhenghe year two, at the ironworks in Zhuo Commandery, while iron was being cast, the molten metal flew upward and away; this happened as a portentary transformation of the element fire.

sentences.zh: 其三月，涿郡太守劉屈釐為丞相。

translations.literal: That third month, Zhuo commandery governor Liu Quli became chancellor.
translations.idiomatic: That third month, Zhuo commandery governor Liu Quli became chancellor.

sentences.zh: 後月，巫蠱事興，帝女諸邑公主、陽石公主、丞相公孫賀、子太僕敬聲、平陽侯曹宗等皆下獄死。

translations.literal: The next month, the witchcraft affair erupted: imperial daughters Princess Zhuyi and Princess Yangshi, Chancellor Gongsun He, his son Grand Coachman Jingsheng, Pingyang marquis Cao Zong and others were imprisoned and died.
translations.idiomatic: The next month, the witchcraft affair erupted: imperial daughters Princess Zhuyi and Princess Yangshi, Chancellor Gongsun He, his son Grand Coachman Jingsheng, Pingyang marquis Cao Zong and others were imprisoned and died.

sentences.zh: 七月，使者江充掘蠱太子宮，太子與母皇后議，恐不能自明，乃殺充，舉兵與丞相劉屈釐戰，死者數萬人，太子敗走，至湖自殺。

translations.literal: In seventh month envoy Jiang Chong dug for curses in Crown Prince's palace; the prince and his mother empress, fearing they could not clear themselves, killed Chong, raised troops, fought Chancellor Liu Quli, tens of thousands died, and the prince fled in defeat and killed himself at Hu.
translations.idiomatic: In seventh month envoy Jiang Chong dug for curses in Crown Prince's palace; the prince and his mother empress, fearing they could not clear themselves, killed Chong, raised troops, fought Chancellor Liu Quli, tens of thousands died, and the prince fled in defeat and killed himself at Hu.

sentences.zh: 明年，屈釐復坐祝验要斬，妻梟首也。

translations.literal: The next year Quli was again convicted in curse-case and beheaded at waist; his wife was decapitated and displayed.
translations.idiomatic: The next year Quli was again convicted in curse-case and beheaded at waist; his wife was decapitated and displayed.

sentences.zh: 成帝河平二年正月，沛郡鐵官鑄鐵，鐵不下，隆隆如雷聲，又如鼓音，工十三人驚走。

translations.literal: In Emperor Cheng Heping year two first month, at Pei commandery iron office, cast iron would not descend; it thundered like drums and thirteen workers fled in fright.
translations.idiomatic: In the first month of Heping year two under Emperor Cheng, at the ironworks in Pei Commandery, molten iron would not flow downward; it boomed like thunder, like drumbeats, and thirteen workers fled in alarm.

sentences.zh: 音止，還視地，地陷數尺，鑪分為十，一鑪中銷鐵散如流星，皆上去，與征和二年同象。

translations.literal: When the sound stopped and they looked back, the ground had sunk several chi, the furnace split into ten, and molten iron in one furnace scattered like meteors, all flying upward - same image as Zhenghe year two.
translations.idiomatic: When the sound stopped and they looked back, the ground had sunk several chi, the furnace split into ten, and molten iron in one furnace scattered like meteors, all flying upward - same image as Zhenghe year two.

sentences.zh: 其夏，帝舅五人封列侯，號五侯。

translations.literal: That summer, five imperial uncles were enfeoffed as marquises, called the Five Marquises.
translations.idiomatic: That summer, five imperial uncles were enfeoffed as marquises, called the Five Marquises.

sentences.zh: 元舅王鳳為大司馬大將軍秉政。

translations.literal: Senior uncle Wang Feng became Grand Marshal and Grand General, holding state power.
translations.idiomatic: Senior uncle Wang Feng became Grand Marshal and Grand General, holding state power.

sentences.zh: 後二年，丞相王商與鳳有隙，鳳譖之，免官，自殺。

translations.literal: Two years later Chancellor Wang Shang had friction with Feng; Feng slandered him, he was removed and killed himself.
translations.idiomatic: Two years later Chancellor Wang Shang had friction with Feng; Feng slandered him, he was removed and killed himself.

sentences.zh: 明年，京兆尹王章訟商忠直，言鳳顓權，鳳誣章以大逆罪，下獄死，妻子徙合浦。

translations.literal: Next year Capital Governor Wang Zhang defended Shang's loyalty and uprightness and said Feng monopolized power; Feng falsely accused Zhang of treason, who died in prison, and his wife and children were exiled to Hepu.
translations.idiomatic: Next year Capital Governor Wang Zhang defended Shang's loyalty and uprightness and said Feng monopolized power; Feng falsely accused Zhang of treason, who died in prison, and his wife and children were exiled to Hepu.

sentences.zh: 後許皇后坐巫蠱廢，而趙飛燕為皇后，妹為昭儀，賊害皇子，成帝遂亡嗣。

translations.literal: Later Empress Xu was deposed in witchcraft case; Zhao Feiyan became empress and her sister became imperial consort; they harmed imperial sons, and Emperor Cheng ultimately had no heir.
translations.idiomatic: Later Empress Xu was deposed in witchcraft case; Zhao Feiyan became empress and her sister became imperial consort; they harmed imperial sons, and Emperor Cheng ultimately had no heir.

sentences.zh: 皇后，昭儀皆伏辜。

translations.literal: Both empress and consort were later punished.
translations.idiomatic: Both empress and consort were later punished.

sentences.zh: 一曰，鐵飛屬金不從革。

translations.literal: Another view: iron flying corresponds to metal not yielding to reform.
translations.idiomatic: Another view: iron flying corresponds to metal not yielding to reform.

sentences.zh: 元鳳四年五月丁丑，孝文廟正殿災。

translations.literal: In Yuanfeng year four, on dingchou day of fifth month, fire struck main hall of Emperor Wen's temple.
translations.idiomatic: In Yuanfeng year four, on dingchou day of fifth month, fire struck main hall of Emperor Wen's temple.

sentences.zh: 劉向以為孝文，太宗之君，與成周宣榭火同義。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held that Emperor Wen was a temple ruler of Taizong class, same meaning as fire at Xuanshe in Chengzhou.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held that Emperor Wen was a temple ruler of Taizong class, same meaning as fire at Xuanshe in Chengzhou.

sentences.zh: 先是，皇后父車騎將軍上官安、安父左將軍桀謀為逆，大將軍霍光誅之。

translations.literal: Before this, empress's father, Chariot-Cavalry General Shangguan An, and his father Left General Jie plotted treason; Grand General Huo Guang executed them.
translations.idiomatic: Before this, empress's father, Chariot-Cavalry General Shangguan An, and his father Left General Jie plotted treason; Grand General Huo Guang executed them.

sentences.zh: 皇后以光外孫，年少不知，居位如故。

translations.literal: The empress, as Guang's external granddaughter, was young and ignorant and remained in position as before.
translations.idiomatic: The empress, as Guang's external granddaughter, was young and ignorant and remained in position as before.

sentences.zh: 光欲后有子，因上侍疾醫言，禁內後宮皆不得進，唯皇后顓寢。

translations.literal: Guang wanted the empress to have a son, and through physicians attending the emperor's illness barred all inner-palace women from entry, leaving only the empress to attend sleeping quarters.
translations.idiomatic: Guang wanted the empress to have a son, and through physicians attending the emperor's illness barred all inner-palace women from entry, leaving only the empress to attend sleeping quarters.

sentences.zh: 皇后年六歲而立，十三年而昭帝崩，遂絕繼嗣。

translations.literal: The empress was enthroned at age six; after thirteen years Emperor Zhao died, and succession was cut off.
translations.idiomatic: The empress was enthroned at age six; after thirteen years Emperor Zhao died, and succession was cut off.

sentences.zh: 光執朝政，猶周公之攝也。

translations.literal: Guang held court power, as the Duke of Zhou had once acted as regent.
translations.idiomatic: Guang held court power, as the Duke of Zhou had once acted as regent.

sentences.zh: 是歲正月，上加元服，通詩、尚書，有明悊之性。

translations.literal: That year in first month the emperor received adult cap; he understood Odes and Documents and had a clear and wise nature.
translations.idiomatic: That year in first month the emperor received adult cap; he understood Odes and Documents and had a clear and wise nature.

sentences.zh: 光亡周公之德，秉政九年，久於周公，上既已冠而不歸政，將為國害。

translations.literal: Guang lacked Duke Zhou's virtue; holding power nine years, longer than Zhou Gong, and after the emperor was already crowned still not returning authority, he was becoming a danger to the state.
translations.idiomatic: Guang lacked Duke Zhou's virtue; holding power nine years, longer than Zhou Gong, and after the emperor was already crowned still not returning authority, he was becoming a danger to the state.

sentences.zh: 故正月加元服，五月而災見。

translations.literal: Therefore the cap ceremony was in first month, and by fifth month the disaster sign appeared.
translations.idiomatic: Therefore the cap ceremony was in first month, and by fifth month the disaster sign appeared.

sentences.zh: 古之廟皆在城中，孝文廟始出居外，天戒若曰，去貴而不正者。

translations.literal: Ancient temples were all inside city; Emperor Wen's temple was first moved outside. Heaven's warning was as if saying: remove those honored yet unrectified.
translations.idiomatic: Ancient temples were all inside city; Emperor Wen's temple was first moved outside. Heaven's warning was as if saying: remove those honored yet unrectified.

sentences.zh: 宣帝既立，光猶攝政，驕溢過制，至妻顯殺許皇后，光聞而不討，後遂誅滅。

translations.literal: After Emperor Xuan was established, Guang still held regency and overstepped in arrogance, even to the point his wife Xian killed Empress Xu; Guang heard and did not prosecute, and later the clan was exterminated.
