translations.idiomatic: After Emperor Xuan was established, Guang still held regency and overstepped in arrogance, even to the point his wife Xian killed Empress Xu; Guang heard and did not prosecute, and later the clan was exterminated.

sentences.zh: 宣帝甘露元年四月丙申，中山太上皇廟災。

translations.literal: In Emperor Xuan Ganlu year one, on bingshen day of fourth month, fire struck Supreme Emperor's temple in Zhongshan.
translations.idiomatic: In Emperor Xuan Ganlu year one, on bingshen day of fourth month, fire struck Supreme Emperor's temple in Zhongshan.

sentences.zh: 甲辰，孝文廟災。

translations.literal: On jiachen day, Emperor Wen's temple burned.
translations.idiomatic: On jiachen day, Emperor Wen's temple burned.

sentences.zh: 元帝初元三年四月乙未，孝武園白鶴館災。

translations.literal: In Emperor Yuan Chuyuan year three, on yiwei day of fourth month, White Crane Hall in Emperor Wu's garden burned.
translations.idiomatic: In Emperor Yuan Chuyuan year three, on yiwei day of fourth month, White Crane Hall in Emperor Wu's garden burned.

sentences.zh: 劉向以為先是前將軍蕭望之、光祿大夫周堪輔政，為佞臣石顯、許章等所譖，望之自殺，堪廢黜。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held that before this, Former General Xiao Wangzhi and Grandee Zhou Kan had assisted government, but were slandered by treacherous ministers Shi Xian and Xu Zhang; Wangzhi killed himself and Kan was dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held that before this, Former General Xiao Wangzhi and Grandee Zhou Kan had assisted government, but were slandered by treacherous ministers Shi Xian and Xu Zhang; Wangzhi killed himself and Kan was dismissed.

sentences.zh: 明年，白鶴館災。

translations.literal: In the following year, White Crane Hall burned.
translations.idiomatic: In the following year, White Crane Hall burned.

sentences.zh: 園中五里馳逐走馬之館，不當在山陵昭穆之地。

translations.literal: A hall in the garden for five-li horse racing and chasing should not have been in the sacred mausoleum alignment grounds.
translations.idiomatic: A hall in the garden for five-li horse racing and chasing should not have been in the sacred mausoleum alignment grounds.

sentences.zh: 天戒若曰，去貴近逸遊不正之臣，將害忠良。

translations.literal: Heaven's warning was: remove intimate high-ranking pleasure-seeking ministers who are unrectified and will harm the loyal and good.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven's warning was: remove intimate high-ranking pleasure-seeking ministers who are unrectified and will harm the loyal and good.

sentences.zh: 後章坐走馬上林下烽馳逐，免官。

translations.literal: Later Xu Zhang was removed for racing horses and beacon-chasing at Shanglin.
translations.idiomatic: Later Xu Zhang was removed for racing horses and beacon-chasing at Shanglin.

sentences.zh: 永光四年六月甲戌，孝宣杜陵園東闕南方災。

translations.literal: In Yongguang year four, on jiaxu day of sixth month, fire struck south side of east gate tower at Emperor Xuan's Duling garden.
translations.idiomatic: In Yongguang year four, on jiaxu day of sixth month, fire struck south side of east gate tower at Emperor Xuan's Duling garden.

sentences.zh: 劉向以為先是上復徵用周堪為光祿勳，及堪弟子張猛為太中大夫，石顯等復譖毀之，皆出外遷。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held that before this the emperor had recalled Zhou Kan as Minister of Household and Kan's disciple Zhang Meng as Grandee; Shi Xian and others again slandered them, and all were sent out and transferred.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held that before this the emperor had recalled Zhou Kan as Minister of Household and Kan's disciple Zhang Meng as Grandee; Shi Xian and others again slandered them, and all were sent out and transferred.

sentences.zh: 是歲，上復徵堪領尚書，猛給事中，石顯等終欲害之。

translations.literal: That year the emperor again summoned Kan to head Secretariat and Meng to Palace Service; Shi Xian and others still sought to harm them.
translations.idiomatic: That year the emperor again summoned Kan to head Secretariat and Meng to Palace Service; Shi Xian and others still sought to harm them.

sentences.zh: 園陵小於朝廷，闕在司馬門中，內臣石顯之象也。

translations.literal: Garden mausoleum is lesser than court; its gate tower within commander gate symbolizes inner minister Shi Xian.
translations.idiomatic: Garden mausoleum is lesser than court; its gate tower within commander gate symbolizes inner minister Shi Xian.

sentences.zh: 孝宣，親而貴；

translations.literal: Emperor Xuan was intimate and honored.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Xuan was intimate and honored.

sentences.zh: 闕，法令所從出也。

translations.literal: Gate tower is where laws and commands issue.
translations.idiomatic: Gate tower is where laws and commands issue.

sentences.zh: 天戒若曰，去法令，內臣親而貴者必為國害。

translations.literal: Heaven's warning was: if legal commands are controlled by intimate and honored inner ministers, they will surely harm the state.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven's warning was: if legal commands are controlled by intimate and honored inner ministers, they will surely harm the state.

sentences.zh: 後堪希得進見，因顯言事，事決顯口。

translations.literal: Later Kan could scarcely gain audience; matters were decided through Xian's mouth.
translations.idiomatic: Later Kan could scarcely gain audience; matters were decided through Xian's mouth.

sentences.zh: 堪病不能言。

translations.literal: Kan became ill and could not speak.
translations.idiomatic: Kan became ill and could not speak.

sentences.zh: 顯誣告張猛，自殺於公車。

translations.literal: Xian falsely accused Zhang Meng, who killed himself at public carriage office.
translations.idiomatic: Xian falsely accused Zhang Meng, who killed himself at public carriage office.

sentences.zh: 成帝即位，顯卒伏辜。

translations.literal: When Emperor Cheng took the throne, Xian eventually suffered punishment.
translations.idiomatic: When Emperor Cheng took the throne, Xian eventually suffered punishment.

sentences.zh: 成帝建始元年正月乙丑，皇考廟災。

translations.literal: In Emperor Cheng Jianshi year one, on yichou day of first month, fire struck the temple of the imperial father.
translations.idiomatic: In Emperor Cheng Jianshi year one, on yichou day of first month, fire struck the temple of the imperial father.

sentences.zh: 初，宣帝為昭帝後而立父廟，於禮不正。

translations.literal: Originally Emperor Xuan was established as successor to Emperor Zhao and then set up his father's temple, which was ritually improper.
translations.idiomatic: Originally Emperor Xuan was established as successor to Emperor Zhao and then set up his father's temple, which was ritually improper.

sentences.zh: 是時大將軍王鳳顓權擅朝，甚於田蚡，將害國家，故天於元年正月而見象也。

translations.literal: At this time Grand General Wang Feng monopolized court power, even more than Tian Fen, and would harm the state; therefore Heaven showed the sign in first month of first year.
translations.idiomatic: At this time Grand General Wang Feng monopolized court power, even more than Tian Fen, and would harm the state; therefore Heaven showed the sign in first month of first year.

sentences.zh: 其後寖盛，五將世權，遂以亡道。

translations.literal: Afterward it grew ever stronger, with five generals holding hereditary power, and the state finally fell into lawlessness.
translations.idiomatic: Afterward it grew ever stronger, with five generals holding hereditary power, and the state finally fell into lawlessness.

sentences.zh: 鴻嘉三年八月乙卯，孝景廟北闕災。

translations.literal: In Hongjia year three, on yimao day of eighth month, fire struck the north gate tower of Emperor Jing's temple.
translations.idiomatic: In Hongjia year three, on yimao day of eighth month, fire struck the north gate tower of Emperor Jing's temple.

sentences.zh: 十一月甲寅，許皇后廢。

translations.literal: On jiayin day of eleventh month, Empress Xu was deposed.
translations.idiomatic: On jiayin day of eleventh month, Empress Xu was deposed.

sentences.zh: 永始四年四月癸未，長樂宮臨華殿及未央宮東司馬門災。

translations.literal: In Yongshi year four, on guiwei day of fourth month, Linhua Hall in Changle Palace and east Sima Gate of Weiyang Palace burned.
translations.idiomatic: In Yongshi year four, on guiwei day of fourth month, Linhua Hall in Changle Palace and east Sima Gate of Weiyang Palace burned.

sentences.zh: 六月甲午，孝文霸陵園東闕南方災。

translations.literal: On jiamao day of sixth month, fire struck south side of east gate tower in Emperor Wen's Baling garden.
translations.idiomatic: On jiamao day of sixth month, fire struck south side of east gate tower in Emperor Wen's Baling garden.

sentences.zh: 長樂宮，成帝母王太后之所居也。

translations.literal: Changle Palace was residence of Emperor Cheng's mother, Empress Dowager Wang.
translations.idiomatic: Changle Palace was residence of Emperor Cheng's mother, Empress Dowager Wang.

sentences.zh: 未央宮，帝所居也。

translations.literal: Weiyang Palace was emperor's residence.
translations.idiomatic: Weiyang Palace was emperor's residence.

sentences.zh: 霸陵，太宗盛德園也。

translations.literal: Baling was the garden of Taizong's flourishing virtue.
translations.idiomatic: Baling was the garden of Taizong's flourishing virtue.

sentences.zh: 是時，太后三弟相續秉政，舉宗居位，充塞朝廷，兩宮親屬將害國家，故天象仍見。

translations.literal: At this time the Empress Dowager's three younger brothers successively held power; their whole clan occupied positions and filled court. Relatives of both palaces were about to harm state, so heavenly signs repeatedly appeared.
translations.idiomatic: At this time the Empress Dowager's three younger brothers successively held power; their whole clan occupied positions and filled court. Relatives of both palaces were about to harm state, so heavenly signs repeatedly appeared.

sentences.zh: 明年，成都侯商薨，弟曲陽侯根代為大司馬秉政。

translations.literal: Next year Chengdu marquis Shang died, and his younger brother Quyang marquis Gen replaced him as Grand Marshal in control of government.
translations.idiomatic: Next year Chengdu marquis Shang died, and his younger brother Quyang marquis Gen replaced him as Grand Marshal in control of government.

sentences.zh: 後四年，根乞骸骨，薦兄子新都侯莽自代，遂覆國焉。

translations.literal: Four years later Gen requested retirement and recommended his nephew, Xindu marquis Mang, to replace him, who then overturned the state.
translations.idiomatic: Four years later Gen requested retirement and recommended his nephew, Xindu marquis Mang, to replace him, who then overturned the state.

sentences.zh: 哀帝建平三年正月癸卯，桂宮鴻寧殿災，帝祖母傅太后之所居也。

translations.literal: In Emperor Ai Jianping year three, on guimao day of first month, Hongning Hall in Gui Palace burned, residence of Emperor Ai's grandmother, Empress Dowager Fu.
translations.idiomatic: On the guimao day of the first month in Jianping year three under Emperor Ai, Hongning Hall in Gui Palace caught fire; it was the residence of Emperor Ai's grandmother, Empress Dowager Fu.

sentences.zh: 時，傅太后欲與成帝母等號齊尊，大臣孔光、師丹等執政，以為不可，太后皆免官爵，遂稱尊號。

translations.literal: At that time Empress Dowager Fu wanted equal honor-title with Emperor Cheng's mother; ministers Kong Guang and Shi Dan opposed it, saying it was improper. She stripped them of rank and office and then took the exalted title.
translations.idiomatic: At that time Empress Dowager Fu wanted equal honor-title with Emperor Cheng's mother; ministers Kong Guang and Shi Dan opposed it, saying it was improper. She stripped them of rank and office and then took the exalted title.

sentences.zh: 後三年，帝崩，傅氏誅滅。

translations.literal: Three years later the emperor died and the Fu clan was exterminated.
translations.idiomatic: Three years later the emperor died and the Fu clan was exterminated.

sentences.zh: 平帝元始五年七月己亥，高皇帝原廟殿門災盡。

translations.literal: In Emperor Ping Yuanshi year five, on jihai day of seventh month, the temple gate hall of Gao Emperor's original shrine burned completely.
translations.idiomatic: In Emperor Ping Yuanshi year five, on jihai day of seventh month, the temple gate hall of Gao Emperor's original shrine burned completely.

sentences.zh: 高皇帝廟在長安城中，後以叔孫通譏復道，故復起原廟於渭北，非正也。

translations.literal: Emperor Gao's ancestral temple was inside the city of Chang'an. Later, because Shusun Tong criticized the raised passageway, they again established the original temple north of the Wei; this was not proper.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Gao's ancestral temple was inside the city of Chang'an. Later, because Shusun Tong criticized the raised passageway, they again established the original temple north of the Wei; this was not proper.

sentences.zh: 是時平帝幼，成帝母王太后臨朝，委任王莽，將篡絕漢，墮高祖宗廟，故天象見也。

translations.literal: At that time Emperor Ping was young, and Empress Dowager Wang, mother of Emperor Cheng, ruled at court and entrusted power to Wang Mang, who was about to usurp and cut off the Han and cast down Gaozu's ancestral temple; therefore this heavenly sign appeared.
translations.idiomatic: At that time Emperor Ping was young, and Empress Dowager Wang, mother of Emperor Cheng, ruled at court and entrusted power to Wang Mang, who was about to usurp and cut off the Han and cast down Gaozu's ancestral temple; therefore this heavenly sign appeared.

sentences.zh: 其冬，平帝崩。

translations.literal: That winter, Emperor Ping died.
translations.idiomatic: That winter, Emperor Ping died.

sentences.zh: 明年，莽居攝，因以篡國，後卒夷滅。

translations.literal: The next year, Mang acted as regent, and thereby usurped the state; in the end he was exterminated.
translations.idiomatic: The next year, Mang acted as regent, and thereby usurped the state; in the end he was exterminated.

sentences.zh: 傳曰：「治宮室，飾臺榭，內淫亂，犯親戚，侮父兄，則稼穡不成。」

translations.literal: The Tradition says: "If one overbuilds palaces and adorns terraces and pavilions, is licentious in the inner quarters, offends kin, and insults fathers and elder brothers, then husbandry will fail."
translations.idiomatic: The Tradition says: "If one overbuilds palaces and adorns terraces and pavilions, is licentious in the inner quarters, offends kin, and insults fathers and elder brothers, then husbandry will fail."

sentences.zh: 說曰：土，中央，生萬物者也。

translations.literal: The explanation says: Earth is the center, the one that gives birth to all things.
translations.idiomatic: The explanation says: Earth is the center, the one that gives birth to all things.

sentences.zh: 其於王者，為內事。

translations.literal: In relation to kingship, it governs inner affairs.
translations.idiomatic: In relation to kingship, it governs inner affairs.

sentences.zh: 宮室、夫婦、親屬，亦相生者也。

translations.literal: Palaces, husband and wife, and kin relations likewise generate one another.
translations.idiomatic: Palaces, husband and wife, and kin relations likewise generate one another.

sentences.zh: 古者天子諸侯，宮廟大小高卑有制，后夫人媵妾多少進退有度，九族親疏長幼有序。

translations.literal: In antiquity, for Son of Heaven and feudal lords, there were regulations for the size, height, and rank of palaces and ancestral temples; queens, consorts, and concubines had fixed numbers and proper order of advancement and withdrawal; and among the nine kin groups, closeness and distance, elder and younger, all had sequence.
translations.idiomatic: In antiquity, for Son of Heaven and feudal lords, there were regulations for the size, height, and rank of palaces and ancestral temples; queens, consorts, and concubines had fixed numbers and proper order of advancement and withdrawal; and among the nine kin groups, closeness and distance, elder and younger, all had sequence.

sentences.zh: 孔子曰：「禮，與其奢也，寧儉。」

translations.literal: Confucius said, "In ritual, rather than extravagance, frugality is better."
translations.idiomatic: Confucius said, "In ritual, rather than extravagance, frugality is better."

sentences.zh: 故禹卑宮室，文王刑于寡妻，此聖人之所以昭教化也。

translations.literal: Therefore Yu kept his palaces low, and King Wen set order beginning with his own wife; this is how sages made moral transformation manifest.
translations.idiomatic: Therefore Yu kept his palaces low, and King Wen set order beginning with his own wife; this is how sages made moral transformation manifest.

sentences.zh: 如此則土得其性矣。

translations.literal: In this way Earth obtains its proper nature.
translations.idiomatic: In this way Earth obtains its proper nature.

sentences.zh: 若乃奢淫驕慢，則土失其性。

translations.literal: But if there is extravagance, licentiousness, arrogance, and pride, then Earth loses its proper nature.
translations.idiomatic: But if there is extravagance, licentiousness, arrogance, and pride, then Earth loses its proper nature.

sentences.zh: 亡水旱之災而草木百穀不孰，是為稼穡不成。

translations.literal: When there is no flood or drought disaster yet grasses, trees, and the hundred grains do not ripen, this is called failure of husbandry.
translations.idiomatic: When there is no flood or drought disaster yet grasses, trees, and the hundred grains do not ripen, this is called failure of husbandry.

sentences.zh: 嚴公二十八年「冬，大水亡麥禾」。

translations.literal: In Duke Zhuang year twenty-eight: "In winter, great flooding destroyed wheat and grain."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Zhuang year twenty-eight: "In winter, great flooding destroyed wheat and grain."

sentences.zh: 董仲舒以為夫人哀姜淫亂，逆陰氣，故大水也。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu considered that Madam Ai Jiang was licentious and disordered, reversing yin qi, therefore there was great flooding.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu considered that Madam Ai Jiang was licentious and disordered, reversing yin qi, therefore there was great flooding.

sentences.zh: 劉向以為水旱當書，不書水旱而曰「

translations.literal: Liu Xiang considered that droughts and floods should be recorded; instead of recording drought or flood, it says,
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang considered that droughts and floods should be recorded; instead of recording drought or flood, it says,

sentences.zh: 大亡麥禾」者，土氣不養，稼穡不成者也。

translations.literal: "Great loss of wheat and grain," meaning Earth's qi did not nourish and husbandry failed.
translations.idiomatic: "Great loss of wheat and grain," meaning Earth's qi did not nourish and husbandry failed.

sentences.zh: 是時，夫人淫於二叔，內外亡別，又因凶飢，一年而三築臺，故應是而稼穡不成，飾臺榭內淫亂之罰云。

translations.literal: At this time the lady was licentious with the two uncles, inside and outside had no distinction, and amid famine they built terraces three times in one year; thus this response appeared as the punishment for adorning terraces and pavilions and inner licentious disorder, with failed husbandry.
translations.idiomatic: At this time the lady was licentious with the two uncles, inside and outside had no distinction, and amid famine they built terraces three times in one year; thus this response appeared as the punishment for adorning terraces and pavilions and inner licentious disorder, with failed husbandry.

sentences.zh: 遂不改寤，四年而死，禍流二世，奢淫之患也。

translations.literal: Yet they did not change or awaken; four years later he died, and the calamity flowed into two generations: this was the harm of extravagance and licentiousness.
translations.idiomatic: Yet they did not change or awaken; four years later he died, and the calamity flowed into two generations: this was the harm of extravagance and licentiousness.

sentences.zh: 傳曰：「好戰攻，輕百姓，飾城郭，侵邊境，則金不從革。」

translations.literal: The Tradition says: "If one delights in war and attack, treats the people lightly, embellishes city walls, and encroaches on borders, then Metal will not follow its proper reforming nature."
translations.idiomatic: The Tradition says: "If one delights in war and attack, treats the people lightly, embellishes city walls, and encroaches on borders, then Metal will not follow its proper reforming nature."

sentences.zh: 說曰：金，西方，萬物既成，殺氣之始也。

translations.literal: The explanation says: Metal belongs to the west; when all things are already formed, it is the beginning of killing qi.
translations.idiomatic: The explanation says: Metal belongs to the west; when all things are already formed, it is the beginning of killing qi.

sentences.zh: 故立秋而鷹隼擊，秋分而微霜降。

translations.literal: Therefore at the start of autumn hawks and falcons strike, and at the autumn equinox light frost descends.
translations.idiomatic: Therefore at the start of autumn hawks and falcons strike, and at the autumn equinox light frost descends.

sentences.zh: 其於王事，出軍行師，把旄杖鉞，誓士眾，抗威武，所以征畔逆止暴亂也。

translations.literal: In royal affairs it means sending out armies and conducting campaigns, bearing plumes and battle-axes, swearing the troops, and displaying martial authority, in order to punish rebellion and stop violence and disorder.
translations.idiomatic: In royal affairs it means sending out armies and conducting campaigns, bearing plumes and battle-axes, swearing the troops, and displaying martial authority, in order to punish rebellion and stop violence and disorder.

sentences.zh: 《詩》云：「有虔秉鉞，如火烈烈。」

translations.literal: The Odes says: "Reverently bearing the battle-axe, blazing like fierce fire."
translations.idiomatic: The Odes says: "Reverently bearing the battle-axe, blazing like fierce fire."

sentences.zh: 又曰：「載戢干戈，載櫜弓矢。」

translations.literal: It also says: "Then we sheathe spears and halberds, then bag bows and arrows."
translations.idiomatic: It also says: "Then we sheathe spears and halberds, then bag bows and arrows."

sentences.zh: 動靜應誼，「說以犯難，民忘其死。」

translations.literal: Movement and stillness accord with rightness: "By speaking of facing peril, the people forget death."
translations.idiomatic: Movement and stillness accord with rightness: "By speaking of facing peril, the people forget death."

sentences.zh: 金得其性矣。

translations.literal: Then Metal obtains its proper nature.
translations.idiomatic: Then Metal obtains its proper nature.

sentences.zh: 若乃貪欲恣睢，務立威勝，不重民命，則金失其性。

translations.literal: But if there is greedy desire and reckless indulgence, with effort spent on asserting power and victory and no regard for people's lives, then Metal loses its proper nature.
translations.idiomatic: But if there is greedy desire and reckless indulgence, with effort spent on asserting power and victory and no regard for people's lives, then Metal loses its proper nature.

sentences.zh: 蓋工冶鑄金鐵，金鐵冰滯涸堅，不成者眾，及為變怪，是為金不從革。

translations.literal: When artisans and smiths cast gold and iron, and the metal congeals, stagnates, and hardens so that many castings fail, and then strange anomalies appear, this is called Metal not following reform.
translations.idiomatic: When artisans and smiths cast gold and iron, and the metal congeals, stagnates, and hardens so that many castings fail, and then strange anomalies appear, this is called Metal not following reform.

sentences.zh: 左氏傳曰昭公八年「春，石言於晉」。

translations.literal: The Zuo Tradition says in Duke Zhao year eight: "In spring, a stone spoke in Jin."
translations.idiomatic: The Zuo Tradition says in Duke Zhao year eight: "In spring, a stone spoke in Jin."

sentences.zh: 晉平公問於師曠，對曰；

translations.literal: Duke Ping of Jin asked Shi Kuang, who answered:
translations.idiomatic: Duke Ping of Jin asked Shi Kuang, who answered:

sentences.zh: 「石不能言，神或馮焉。

translations.literal: "Stone cannot speak; perhaps a spirit relied on it.
translations.idiomatic: "Stone cannot speak; perhaps a spirit relied on it.

sentences.zh: 作事不時，怨讟動於民，則有非言之物而言。

translations.literal: When actions are mistimed and resentment and slander stir among the people, then things that should not speak will speak.
translations.idiomatic: When actions are mistimed and resentment and slander stir among the people, then things that should not speak will speak.

sentences.zh: 今宮室崇侈，民力彫盡，怨讟並興，莫信其性，石之言不宜乎！」

translations.literal: Now palaces are lofty and extravagant, the people's strength is exhausted, resentment and slander rise together, and none trust what is normal; is it not fitting that stone should speak?"
translations.idiomatic: Now palaces are lofty and extravagant, the people's strength is exhausted, resentment and slander rise together, and none trust what is normal; is it not fitting that stone should speak?"

sentences.zh: 於是晉侯方築虒祁之宮。

translations.literal: At that time the marquis of Jin was building the palace of Siqi.
translations.idiomatic: At that time the marquis of Jin was building the palace of Siqi.

sentences.zh: 叔向曰：「君子之言，信而有徵。」

translations.literal: Shu Xiang said: "A gentleman's words are credible and have proof."
translations.idiomatic: Shu Xiang said: "A gentleman's words are credible and have proof."

sentences.zh: 劉歆以為金石同類，是為金不從革，失其性也。

translations.literal: Liu Xin considered that metal and stone are of one kind; this is Metal not following reform, losing its nature.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin considered that metal and stone are of one kind; this is Metal not following reform, losing its nature.

sentences.zh: 劉向以為石白色為主，屬白祥。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang considered that stone is chiefly white in color and belongs to white portents.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang considered that stone is chiefly white in color and belongs to white portents.

sentences.zh: 成帝鴻嘉三年五月乙亥，天水冀南山大石鳴，聲隆隆如雷，有頃止，聞平襄二百四十里，野雞皆鳴。

translations.literal: In the fifth month, yihai day, of Hongjia year three under Emperor Cheng, a great stone on South Mountain in Ji of Tianshui sounded, rumbling like thunder; after a while it stopped. It was heard 240 li away at Pingxiang, and pheasants in the fields all cried out.
translations.idiomatic: In the fifth month, yihai day, of Hongjia year three under Emperor Cheng, a great stone on South Mountain in Ji of Tianshui sounded, rumbling like thunder; after a while it stopped. It was heard 240 li away at Pingxiang, and pheasants in the fields all cried out.

sentences.zh: 石長丈三尺，廣厚略等，旁著岸脅，去地二百餘丈，民俗名曰石鼓。

translations.literal: The stone was one zhang three chi long, with width and thickness roughly equal, attached beside a cliff face more than two hundred zhang above the ground; local people called it the Stone Drum.
translations.idiomatic: The stone was one zhang three chi long, with width and thickness roughly equal, attached beside a cliff face more than two hundred zhang above the ground; local people called it the Stone Drum.

sentences.zh: 石鼓鳴，有兵。

translations.literal: When the Stone Drum sounds, there will be warfare.
translations.idiomatic: When the Stone Drum sounds, there will be warfare.

sentences.zh: 是歲，廣漢鉗子謀攻牢，篡死罪囚鄭躬等，盜庫兵，劫略吏民，衣繡衣，自號曰山君，黨與娅廣。

translations.literal: That year Qianzi of Guanghan plotted to attack prison compounds, recruited death-row convict Zheng Gong and others, stole weapons from the armory, robbed officials and commoners, wore embroidered uniforms, called himself Lord of the Mountain, and his followers spread widely.
translations.idiomatic: That year Qianzi of Guanghan plotted to attack prison compounds, recruited death-row convict Zheng Gong and others, stole weapons from the armory, robbed officials and commoners, wore embroidered uniforms, called himself Lord of the Mountain, and his followers spread widely.

sentences.zh: 明年冬，乃伏誅，自歸者三千餘人。

translations.literal: In the following winter he was executed; more than three thousand who surrendered themselves were pardoned.
translations.idiomatic: In the following winter he was executed; more than three thousand who surrendered themselves were pardoned.

sentences.zh: 後四年，尉氏樊並等謀反，殺陳留太守嚴普，自稱將軍，山陽亡徒蘇令等黨與數百人盜取庫兵，經歷郡國四十餘，皆踰年乃伏誅。

translations.literal: Four years later Fan Bing of Weishi and others plotted rebellion, killed Yan Pu the governor of Chenliu, and proclaimed themselves generals; Su Ling and other fugitives of Shanyang with several hundred accomplices stole armory weapons, passed through more than forty commanderies and kingdoms, and were not executed until more than a year later.
translations.idiomatic: Four years later Fan Bing of Weishi and others plotted rebellion, killed Yan Pu the governor of Chenliu, and proclaimed themselves generals; Su Ling and other fugitives of Shanyang with several hundred accomplices stole armory weapons, passed through more than forty commanderies and kingdoms, and were not executed until more than a year later.

sentences.zh: 是時起昌陵，作者數萬人，徙郡國吏民五千餘戶以奉陵邑。

translations.literal: At this time they were constructing Changling, with tens of thousands of laborers, and moved more than five thousand households of officials and people from commanderies and kingdoms to serve the mausoleum town.
translations.idiomatic: At this time they were constructing Changling, with tens of thousands of laborers, and moved more than five thousand households of officials and people from commanderies and kingdoms to serve the mausoleum town.

sentences.zh: 作治五年不成，乃罷昌陵，還徙家。

translations.literal: After five years of construction it was still unfinished, so Changling was abandoned and the relocated households were sent back.
translations.idiomatic: After five years of construction it was still unfinished, so Changling was abandoned and the relocated households were sent back.

sentences.zh: 石鳴，與晉石言同應，師曠所謂「民力彫盡」，傳云「輕百姓」者也。

translations.literal: The sounding stone answered in the same way as the speaking stone of Jin: this was what Shi Kuang called "the people's strength exhausted," and what the Tradition calls "treating the people lightly."
translations.idiomatic: The sounding stone answered in the same way as the speaking stone of Jin: this was what Shi Kuang called "the people's strength exhausted," and what the Tradition calls "treating the people lightly."

sentences.zh: 虒祁離宮去絳都四十里，昌陵亦在郊野，皆與城郭同占。

translations.literal: The detached palace of Siqi was forty li from the Jin capital of Jiang; Changling too was in the open countryside, and both were omens of the same class as city walls.
translations.idiomatic: The detached palace of Siqi was forty li from the Jin capital of Jiang; Changling too was in the open countryside, and both were omens of the same class as city walls.

sentences.zh: 城郭屬金，宮室屬土，外內之別云。

translations.literal: City walls belong to Metal, while palaces belong to Earth; this marks the distinction between outer and inner.
translations.idiomatic: City walls belong to Metal, while palaces belong to Earth; this marks the distinction between outer and inner.

sentences.zh: 傳曰：「簡宗廟，不禱祠，廢祭祀，逆天時，則水不潤下。」

translations.literal: The Tradition says: "If one neglects ancestral temples, does not pray or sacrifice, abolishes rites, and goes against Heaven's seasons, then Water will not moisten and flow downward."
translations.idiomatic: The Tradition says: "If one neglects ancestral temples, does not pray or sacrifice, abolishes rites, and goes against Heaven's seasons, then Water will not moisten and flow downward."

sentences.zh: 嚴公七年「秋，大水，亡麥苗」。

translations.literal: In Duke Zhuang year seven: "In autumn, great flooding destroyed wheat shoots."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Zhuang year seven: "In autumn, great flooding destroyed wheat shoots."

sentences.zh: 董仲舒、劉向以為嚴母文姜與兄齊襄公淫，共殺威公，嚴釋父讎，復取齊女，未入，先與之淫，一年再出，會於道逆亂，臣下賤之之應也。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu and Liu Xiang considered that Duke Zhuang's mother Wen Jiang had illicit relations with her brother Duke Xiang of Qi and together they killed Duke Huan; Zhuang set aside his father's vengeance and again took a woman of Qi, and before she entered he first had illicit relations with her, and in one year she went out twice, meeting on the road in perverse disorder; this was the response to ministers and inferiors despising him.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu and Liu Xiang considered that Duke Zhuang's mother Wen Jiang had illicit relations with her brother Duke Xiang of Qi and together they killed Duke Huan; Zhuang set aside his father's vengeance and again took a woman of Qi, and before she entered he first had illicit relations with her, and in one year she went out twice, meeting on the road in perverse disorder; this was the response to ministers and inferiors despising him.

sentences.zh: 十一年「秋，宋大水」。

translations.literal: In year eleven: "In autumn, Song suffered great flooding."
translations.idiomatic: In year eleven: "In autumn, Song suffered great flooding."

sentences.zh: 董仲舒以為時魯、宋比年為乘丘、鄑之戰，百姓愁怨，陰氣盛，故二國俱水。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu considered that at that time Lu and Song had year after year fought at Chengqiu and Zi, the people were sorrowful and resentful, yin qi became excessive, and therefore both states were flooded.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu considered that at that time Lu and Song had year after year fought at Chengqiu and Zi, the people were sorrowful and resentful, yin qi became excessive, and therefore both states were flooded.

sentences.zh: 劉向以為時宋愍公驕慢，睹災不改，明年與其臣宋萬博戲，婦人在側，矜而罵萬，萬殺公之應。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang considered that Duke Min of Song at that time was arrogant and did not reform on seeing disaster; the next year he gamed with his minister Song Wan, with women at his side, boasted and insulted Wan, and Wan killed the duke as the response.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang considered that Duke Min of Song at that time was arrogant and did not reform on seeing disaster; the next year he gamed with his minister Song Wan, with women at his side, boasted and insulted Wan, and Wan killed the duke as the response.

sentences.zh: 二十四年，「大水」。

translations.literal: In year twenty-four: "Great flooding."
translations.idiomatic: In year twenty-four: "Great flooding."

sentences.zh: 董仲舒以為夫人哀姜淫亂不婦，陰氣盛也。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu considered that Madam Ai Jiang was licentious and not womanly, so yin qi was excessive.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu considered that Madam Ai Jiang was licentious and not womanly, so yin qi was excessive.

sentences.zh: 劉向以為哀姜初入，公使大夫宗婦見，用幣，又淫於二叔，公弗能禁。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang considered that when Ai Jiang first entered, the duke had great officers' wives receive her and presented gifts, and she was again licentious with the two uncles, and the duke could not restrain it.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang considered that when Ai Jiang first entered, the duke had great officers' wives receive her and presented gifts, and she was again licentious with the two uncles, and the duke could not restrain it.

sentences.zh: 臣下賤之，故是歲、明年仍大水。

translations.literal: Ministers and inferiors despised him; therefore that year and the following year there was repeated great flooding.
translations.idiomatic: Ministers and inferiors despised him; therefore that year and the following year there was repeated great flooding.

sentences.zh: 劉歆以為先是嚴飾宗廟，刻桷丹楹，以夸夫人，簡宗廟之罰也。

translations.literal: Liu Xin considered that earlier Duke Zhuang had extravagantly adorned the ancestral temple, carving rafters and painting pillars in red to boast before his wife; this was punishment for neglecting the ancestral temple.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin considered that earlier Duke Zhuang had extravagantly adorned the ancestral temple, carving rafters and painting pillars in red to boast before his wife; this was punishment for neglecting the ancestral temple.

sentences.zh: 宣公十年「秋大水，飢」。

translations.literal: In Duke Xuan year ten: "In autumn there was great flooding and famine."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Xuan year ten: "In autumn there was great flooding and famine."

sentences.zh: 董仲舒以為時比伐邾取邑，亦見報復，兵讎連結，百姓愁怨。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu considered that at this time there were repeated campaigns against Zhu and seizure of towns, drawing retaliation in return; military grudges were linked without end, and the people were sorrowful and resentful.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu considered that at this time there were repeated campaigns against Zhu and seizure of towns, drawing retaliation in return; military grudges were linked without end, and the people were sorrowful and resentful.

sentences.zh: 劉向以為宣公殺子赤而立，子赤，齊出也，故懼，以濟西田賂齊。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang considered that Duke Xuan killed Zichi and took the throne; Zichi was born of a Qi woman, so the duke was afraid and bribed Qi with fields west of the Ji River.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang considered that Duke Xuan killed Zichi and took the throne; Zichi was born of a Qi woman, so the duke was afraid and bribed Qi with fields west of the Ji River.

sentences.zh: 邾子貜且亦齊出也，而宣比與邾交兵。

translations.literal: Lord Quqie of Zhu was also born of Qi, yet Duke Xuan repeatedly fought Zhu.
translations.idiomatic: Lord Quqie of Zhu was also born of Qi, yet Duke Xuan repeatedly fought Zhu.

sentences.zh: 臣下懼齊之威，創邾之禍，皆賤公行而非其正也。

translations.literal: Ministers and inferiors feared Qi's power and suffered the calamity from Zhu; all despised the duke's conduct and rejected its legitimacy.
translations.idiomatic: Ministers and inferiors feared Qi's power and suffered the calamity from Zhu; all despised the duke's conduct and rejected its legitimacy.

sentences.zh: 成公五年「秋，大水」。

translations.literal: In Duke Cheng year five: "In autumn, great flooding."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Cheng year five: "In autumn, great flooding."

sentences.zh: 董仲舒、劉向以為時成幼弱，政在大夫，前此一年再用師，明年復城鄆以彊私家，仲孫蔑、叔孫僑如顓會宋、晉，陰勝陽。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu and Liu Xiang considered that Duke Cheng was young and weak and government was in great officers' hands; in the previous year troops were used twice, and the next year they again fortified Yun to strengthen private clans, while Zhongsun Mie and Shusun Qiaoru monopolized meetings with Song and Jin; yin overpowered yang.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu and Liu Xiang considered that Duke Cheng was young and weak and government was in great officers' hands; in the previous year troops were used twice, and the next year they again fortified Yun to strengthen private clans, while Zhongsun Mie and Shusun Qiaoru monopolized meetings with Song and Jin; yin overpowered yang.

sentences.zh: 襄公二十四年「秋，大水」。

translations.literal: In Duke Xiang year twenty-four: "In autumn, great flooding."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Xiang year twenty-four: "In autumn, great flooding."

sentences.zh: 董仲舒以為先是一年齊伐晉，襄使大夫帥師救晉，後又侵齊，國小兵弱，數敵彊大，百姓愁怨，陰氣盛。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu considered that in the previous year Qi attacked Jin and Duke Xiang sent great officers leading troops to rescue Jin, and afterward again invaded Qi; the state was small and its army weak, yet it repeatedly opposed powerful great states, and the people were sorrowful and resentful, so yin qi became excessive.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu considered that in the previous year Qi attacked Jin and Duke Xiang sent great officers leading troops to rescue Jin, and afterward again invaded Qi; the state was small and its army weak, yet it repeatedly opposed powerful great states, and the people were sorrowful and resentful, so yin qi became excessive.

sentences.zh: 劉向以為先是襄慢鄰國，是以邾伐其南，齊伐其北，莒伐其東，百姓騷動，後又仍犯彊齊也。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang considered that before this Duke Xiang treated neighboring states arrogantly, so Zhu attacked from the south, Qi from the north, and Ju from the east, and the people were thrown into turmoil; afterward he still offended powerful Qi again.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang considered that before this Duke Xiang treated neighboring states arrogantly, so Zhu attacked from the south, Qi from the north, and Ju from the east, and the people were thrown into turmoil; afterward he still offended powerful Qi again.

sentences.zh: 大水，饑，穀不成，其災甚也。

translations.literal: Great flood and famine, with grain not maturing: the disaster was severe.
translations.idiomatic: Great flood and famine, with grain not maturing: the disaster was severe.

sentences.zh: 高后三年夏，漢中、南郡大水，水出流四千餘家。

translations.literal: In summer of Empress Gao year three, Hanzhong and Nanjun had great floods; waters poured out and swept away more than four thousand households.
translations.idiomatic: In summer of Empress Gao year three, Hanzhong and Nanjun had great floods; waters poured out and swept away more than four thousand households.

sentences.zh: 四年秋，河南大水，伊、雒流千六百餘家，汝水流八百餘家。

translations.literal: In autumn of year four, Henan had great floods: the Yi and Luo rivers swept away more than one thousand six hundred households, and the Ru River swept away more than eight hundred.
translations.idiomatic: In autumn of year four, Henan had great floods: the Yi and Luo rivers swept away more than one thousand six hundred households, and the Ru River swept away more than eight hundred.

sentences.zh: 八年夏，漢中、南郡水復出，流六千餘家。

translations.literal: In summer of year eight, flooding again broke out in Hanzhong and Nanjun, sweeping away more than six thousand households.
translations.idiomatic: In summer of year eight, flooding again broke out in Hanzhong and Nanjun, sweeping away more than six thousand households.

sentences.zh: 南陽沔水流萬餘家。

translations.literal: In Nanyang the Mian River swept away more than ten thousand households.
translations.idiomatic: In Nanyang the Mian River swept away more than ten thousand households.

sentences.zh: 是時女主獨治，諸呂相王。

translations.literal: At this time a female ruler governed alone, and the Lyu clan were all made kings.
translations.idiomatic: At this time a female ruler governed alone, and the Lyu clan were all made kings.

sentences.zh: 文帝後三年秋，大雨，晝夜不絕三十五日。

translations.literal: In autumn of the third year after Emperor Wen's accession, heavy rain fell day and night without cease for thirty-five days.
translations.idiomatic: In autumn of the third year after Emperor Wen's accession, heavy rain fell day and night without cease for thirty-five days.

sentences.zh: 藍田山水出，流九百餘家。

translations.literal: Mountain torrents broke out in Lantian and swept away more than nine hundred households.
translations.idiomatic: Mountain torrents broke out in Lantian and swept away more than nine hundred households.

sentences.zh: 燕，壞民室八千餘所，殺三百餘人。

translations.literal: In Yan, more than eight thousand dwellings were destroyed and more than three hundred people were killed.
translations.idiomatic: In Yan, more than eight thousand dwellings were destroyed and more than three hundred people were killed.

sentences.zh: 先是，趙人新垣平以望氣得幸，為上立渭陽五帝廟，欲出周鼎，以夏四月，郊見上帝。

translations.literal: Previously, Xinyuan Ping of Zhao gained favor by observing qi, established for the emperor the Five Emperors temple at Weiyang, and sought to bring out the Zhou tripod, planning in summer, fourth month, to perform suburban sacrifice and audience with the High Emperor.
translations.idiomatic: Previously, Xinyuan Ping of Zhao gained favor by observing qi, established for the emperor the Five Emperors temple at Weiyang, and sought to bring out the Zhou tripod, planning in summer, fourth month, to perform suburban sacrifice and audience with the High Emperor.

sentences.zh: 歲餘懼誅，謀為逆，發覺，要斬，夷三族。

translations.literal: After more than a year he feared execution, plotted rebellion, was exposed, beheaded at the waist, and his three kin groups were exterminated.
translations.idiomatic: After more than a year he feared execution, plotted rebellion, was exposed, beheaded at the waist, and his three kin groups were exterminated.

sentences.zh: 是時，比再遣公主配單于，賂遺甚厚，匈奴愈驕，侵犯北邊，殺略多至萬餘人，漢連發軍征討戍邊。

translations.literal: At this time the court repeatedly sent princesses to marry the Chanyu and gave very rich gifts, yet the Xiongnu grew more arrogant, invaded the northern frontier, and killed and plundered up to more than ten thousand people; Han repeatedly dispatched armies to campaign and garrison the border.
translations.idiomatic: At this time the court repeatedly sent princesses to marry the Chanyu and gave very rich gifts, yet the Xiongnu grew more arrogant, invaded the northern frontier, and killed and plundered up to more than ten thousand people; Han repeatedly dispatched armies to campaign and garrison the border.

sentences.zh: 元帝永光五年夏及秋，大水。

translations.literal: In summer and autumn of Yongguang year five under Emperor Yuan, there was great flooding.
translations.idiomatic: In summer and autumn of Yongguang year five under Emperor Yuan, there was great flooding.

sentences.zh: 潁川、汝南、淮陽、廬江雨，壞鄉聚民舍，及水流殺人。

translations.literal: In Yingchuan, Runan, Huaiyang, and Lujiang, rains ruined village settlements and commoners' houses, and floodwaters swept people away and killed them.
translations.idiomatic: In Yingchuan, Runan, Huaiyang, and Lujiang, rains ruined village settlements and commoners' houses, and floodwaters swept people away and killed them.

sentences.zh: 先是一年有司奏罷郡國廟，是歲又定迭毀，罷太上皇、孝惠帝寢廟，皆無復修，通儒以為違古制。

translations.literal: In the previous year officials had memorialized to abolish commandery and kingdom temples; this year they again fixed a rotating destruction policy, abolishing the sleeping temples of the Supreme Emperor and Emperor Xiaohui, all left unrepaired. Learned classicists judged this to violate ancient institutions.
translations.idiomatic: In the previous year officials had memorialized to abolish commandery and kingdom temples; this year they again fixed a rotating destruction policy, abolishing the sleeping temples of the Supreme Emperor and Emperor Xiaohui, all left unrepaired. Learned classicists judged this to violate ancient institutions.

sentences.zh: 刑臣石顯用事。

translations.literal: The penal official Shi Xian held power.
translations.idiomatic: The penal official Shi Xian held power.

sentences.zh: 成帝建始三年夏，大水，三輔霖雨三十餘日，郡國十九雨，山谷水出，凡殺四千餘人，壞官寺民舍八萬三千餘所。

translations.literal: In summer of Jianshi year three under Emperor Cheng, there was great flooding: in the Three Adjacent Commanderies rain continued more than thirty days; nineteen commanderies and kingdoms had rain; mountain-valley waters burst out, killing more than four thousand people and destroying over eighty-three thousand government buildings and commoners' homes.
translations.idiomatic: In summer of Jianshi year three under Emperor Cheng, there was great flooding: in the Three Adjacent Commanderies rain continued more than thirty days; nineteen commanderies and kingdoms had rain; mountain-valley waters burst out, killing more than four thousand people and destroying over eighty-three thousand government buildings and commoners' homes.

sentences.zh: 元年，有司奏徙甘泉泰畤、河東后土于長安南北郊。

translations.literal: In year one, officials memorialized to move the Sweet Springs Tai altar and the Hedong Houtu altar to the southern and northern suburbs of Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: In year one, officials memorialized to move the Sweet Springs Tai altar and the Hedong Houtu altar to the southern and northern suburbs of Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 二年，又罷雍五畤、郡國諸舊祀，凡六所。

translations.literal: In year two, they again abolished the five Yong altars and various old sacrifices in the commanderies and kingdoms, six sites in all.
translations.idiomatic: In year two, they again abolished the five Yong altars and various old sacrifices in the commanderies and kingdoms, six sites in all.
